## מַאֲבוּס (id: 003635000000000)

מַאֲבוּס
========

מַאֲבוּס (H3965)

noun m · אבס

Buildings

granary \= building ► used for storing grain

[JER 50:26](https://ref.ly/Jer50:26)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 50:26


## מְאָה (id: 003637000000000)

מְאָה
=====

מְאָה (A3969)

noun

Quantity \| Frequency

hundred \= state in which a countable entity is one hundred in number

[EZR 6:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:17) · [EZR 6:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:17) · [EZR 7:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:22) · [EZR 7:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:22) · [EZR 7:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:22) · [EZR 7:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:22) · [DAN 6:2](https://ref.ly/Dan6:2)

Quantity \| Frequency

**Collocation:** מָאתַיִן

two hundred \= state in which a countable entity is two hundred in number

[EZR 6:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:17)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 6:17; Ezra 7:22; Daniel 6:2

מַאֲוַי
=======

noun m · אוה

## מְגִלָּה (id: 003702000000000)

מְגִלָּה
========

מְגִלָּה (H4039 · A4040) [SAHD](https://sahd-online.com/words/mgillah/)

noun f · גלל

Scrolls

scroll \= long strip of writing material made by sewing together sheets of papyrus or parchment; this strip was then rolled up from one end or from both ends to form one or two cylinders; usually the ends of the scroll were attached to sticks, around which the scroll was rolled; an unrolled scroll was often about 10 meters in length and stood approximately 23–25 centimeters high; the term מְגִלַּת־סֵפֶר seems to be the more complete term for a book scroll, as it occurs at the beginning of passages in which scrolls play a roll; the shorter term מְגִלָּה is used after that

[EZR 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:2) · [PSA 40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:8) · [JER 36:2](https://ref.ly/Jer36:2) · [JER 36:4](https://ref.ly/Jer36:4) · [JER 36:6](https://ref.ly/Jer36:6) · [JER 36:14](https://ref.ly/Jer36:14) · [JER 36:14](https://ref.ly/Jer36:14) · [JER 36:20](https://ref.ly/Jer36:20) · [JER 36:21](https://ref.ly/Jer36:21) · [JER 36:23](https://ref.ly/Jer36:23) · [JER 36:25](https://ref.ly/Jer36:25) · [JER 36:27](https://ref.ly/Jer36:27) · [JER 36:28](https://ref.ly/Jer36:28) · [JER 36:28](https://ref.ly/Jer36:28) · [JER 36:29](https://ref.ly/Jer36:29) · [JER 36:32](https://ref.ly/Jer36:32) · [EZK 2:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:9) · [EZK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:1) · [EZK 3:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:2) · [EZK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:3) · [ZEC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Zech5:1) · [ZEC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Zech5:2)

Reinier de Blois

## מָגֵן (id: 003705000000000)

מָגֵן
=====

מָגֵן (H4043)

noun · גנן

Weapons \| ≈ אַשְׁפָּה · חֲנִיט · חֵץ · חֶרֶב · כֹּובַע · מַקֵּל · צִנָּה · קֶלַע · קֶשֶׁת · רֶכֶב · רֹמַח · שֶׁלֶט · שִׁרְיֹון

shield \= a flat, usually round, object; made of wood or metal; ► used in battle; held by a warrior in front of him as protection from the weapons of his opponent; relatively small and easy to maneuver; ≈ associated with protection and therefore often used as an epithet for God

[JDG 5:8](https://ref.ly/Judg5:8) · [2SA 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:21) · [2SA 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:21) · [1KI 10:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:17) · [1KI 10:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:17) · [1KI 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:26) · [1KI 14:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:27) · [2KI 19:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:32) · [1CH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:18) · [2CH 9:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:16) · [2CH 9:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:16) · [2CH 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:9) · [2CH 12:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:10) · [2CH 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:7) · [2CH 17:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:17) · [2CH 23:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:9) · [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14) · [2CH 32:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:5) · [2CH 32:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27) · [NEH 4:10](https://ref.ly/Neh4:10) · [JOB 15:26](https://ref.ly/Job15:26) · [PSA 35:2](https://ref.ly/Ps35:2) · [PSA 76:4](https://ref.ly/Ps76:4) · [PRO 6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:11) · [PRO 24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:34) · [SNG 4:4](https://ref.ly/Song4:4) · [ISA 21:5](https://ref.ly/Isa21:5) · [ISA 22:6](https://ref.ly/Isa22:6) · [ISA 37:33](https://ref.ly/Isa37:33) · [JER 46:3](https://ref.ly/Jer46:3) · [JER 46:9](https://ref.ly/Jer46:9) · [EZK 23:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:24) · [EZK 27:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:10) · [EZK 38:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:4) · [EZK 38:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:5) · [EZK 39:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:9) · [NAM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Nah2:4)

## מִדְבָּר (id: 003718000000000)

מִדְבָּר
========

מִדְבָּר (H4057)

noun m · דבר

Landscapes \| ≈ עֲרָבָה · צִיָּה

steppe, desert, wilderness \= area not very suitable for sedentary life and agriculture because of its lack of water; suitable for grazing domestic animals and only inhabited by nomadic tribes and wild animals

## מדד (id: 003719000000000)

מדד
===

מדד (H4058 · H4059)

verb · מַד · מִדָּה · מָדוּ · מֵמַד

Measure \| verb qal, verb nif \| ≈ ספר · מִשְׁקָל · תכן

to measure \= action by which humans or deities ascertain the size or quantity of something by comparing it with a standard unit or with an object of known size

Non\-Whole \| verb qal, verb pi

to measure off, to measure out \= causative action by which humans mark off part of a larger unit for a certain purpose

[NUM 35:5](https://ref.ly/Num35:5) · [RUT 3:15](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:15) · [2SA 8:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:2) · [2SA 8:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:2) · [EZK 45:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:3) · [EZK 47:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:3) · [EZK 47:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:4) · [EZK 47:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:4) · [EZK 47:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:5) · [EZK 47:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:18)

Chastise \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מדד עַל־חֵיק

to pay back literally: to measure out on (someone's) lap; hence: \= causative process by which deities cause humans to receive the appropriate return for their evil actions, which are mostly evil

[ISA 65:7](https://ref.ly/Isa65:7)

Non\-Whole \| verb pi \| ≈ חלק

to divide up, to parcel out literally: to measure off; hence: \= action by which deities divide an object into portions in order to share them out among different parties

[PSA 60:8](https://ref.ly/Ps60:8) · [PSA 108:8](https://ref.ly/Ps108:8)

Endure \| verb pi

to last, to drag on \= process by which a certain condition lasts longer than expected or hoped

[JOB 7:4](https://ref.ly/Job7:4)

Stance \| verb hit

stretching oneself out \= action by which humans straighten their body to its full length

[1KI 17:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:21)

## מההּ (id: 003751000000000)

מההּ
====

מההּ (H4102) — מָהַהּ

verb

Non\-Engage \| verb hit

to wait, to linger, to tarry, to dawdle \= action by which humans defer action, either out of indecision or because something else needs to happen first

[GEN 19:16](https://ref.ly/Gen19:16) · [GEN 43:10](https://ref.ly/Gen43:10) · [EXO 12:39](https://ref.ly/Exod12:39) · [JDG 3:26](https://ref.ly/Judg3:26) · [JDG 19:8](https://ref.ly/Judg19:8) · [2SA 15:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:28) · [PSA 119:60](https://ref.ly/Ps119:60)

Delay \| verb hit

to take time \= process by which an event does not take place as soon as expected or desired

[HAB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Hab2:3)

verb · תמהּ

Afraid \| verb hit

to be astonished, to be stupefied, to be confused \= process by which humans are deeply emotionally affected by a difficult situation, to the extent that they do not know how to respond

[ISA 29:9](https://ref.ly/Isa29:9)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 19:16; Genesis 43:10; Exodus 12:39; Judges 3:26; Judges 19:8; 2 Samuel 15:28; Psalms 119:60; Habakkuk 2:3; Isaiah 29:9

Afraid

consternation \= process by which humans are shaken out of a state of complacency and routine, ◄ as a result of a certain event

==========

מַהְפֶּכֶת (H4115)

noun f · הפך

Cords \| ≈ צִינֹק

stocks \= frame with holes for one's feet and possibly wrists as well; ◄ made out of wood; ► used to lock up prisoners so that they cannot move anymore

[JER 20:2](https://ref.ly/Jer20:2) · [JER 20:3](https://ref.ly/Jer20:3) · [JER 29:26](https://ref.ly/Jer29:26)

Prisons

**Collocation:** בֵּית הַמַּהְפֶּכֶת

prison literally: house of stocks; hence: \= building in which a person is kept in captivity, probably with his feet (and wrists) in stocks, usually as punishment for something considered unlawful

[2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 20:2; Jeremiah 20:3; Jeremiah 29:26; 2 Chronicles 16:10

3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)

Afraid \| verb nif

**Collocation:** נִמְהַר־לֵב

to be fearful literally: hastened of heart; hence: \= state in which humans experience an emotion of fear and anxiety which does not allow them to find peace of mind

[ISA 35:4](https://ref.ly/Isa35:4)

Angry \| verb nif \| ≈ מַר

to be rash, to be impetuous literally: hastened; hence: \= state in which humans are inclined to act rashly, out of anger or a lack of patience

[ISA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Isa32:4) · [HAB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hab1:6)

Associate

to run after literally: to hasten (after a deity); hence: \= process by which humans are eager to serve a certain deity

[PSA 16:4](https://ref.ly/Ps16:4)1

Fail \| verb nif

to fail, to go awry literally: to be caused to happen quickly; hence: \= process by which an event fails and comes to a quick end

[JOB 5:13](https://ref.ly/Job5:13)

Happen \| verb pi

to happen soon \= process by which an event happens without delay

[DEU 4:26](https://ref.ly/Deut4:26) · [DEU 28:20](https://ref.ly/Deut28:20) · [PSA 79:8](https://ref.ly/Ps79:8) · [ECC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1) · [ISA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Isa32:4) · [ISA 51:14](https://ref.ly/Isa51:14) · [JER 48:16](https://ref.ly/Jer48:16) · [ZEP 1:14](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:14)

Move \| verb pi

to go quickly, to hasten \= action by which humans or animals move quickly

[GEN 43:30](https://ref.ly/Gen43:30) · [1SA 9:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:12) · [1CH 12:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:9) · [PRO 7:23](https://ref.ly/Prov7:23) · [NAM 2:6](https://ref.ly/Nah2:6)

Move \| verb pi

to bring quickly \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to move quickly

[1KI 22:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:9) · [2CH 18:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:8) · [EST 5:5](https://ref.ly/Esth5:5)

verb · מֹהַר

Associate \| verb qal

to acquire as wife, to marry by paying a bride\-price \= action by which a man makes a woman his wife by paying the bride\-price

[EXO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:15) · [EXO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:15)

## מול (id: 003779000000000)

מול
===

מול (H4135 · H5243b)

verb · מוּלָה

Non\-Whole \| verb qal, verb nif, verb nif

to circumcise, circumcision \= action by which someone removes the foreskin of the penis, \+ preferably with a flint knife, as a sign of the covenant between YHWH and Abraham's descendants; ≈ considered an outward change for the good that should be accompanied by an inward change in attitude as well

[GEN 17:10](https://ref.ly/Gen17:10) · [GEN 17:11](https://ref.ly/Gen17:11) · [GEN 17:12](https://ref.ly/Gen17:12) · [GEN 17:13](https://ref.ly/Gen17:13) · [GEN 17:13](https://ref.ly/Gen17:13) · [GEN 17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:14) · [GEN 17:23](https://ref.ly/Gen17:23) · [GEN 17:24](https://ref.ly/Gen17:24) · [GEN 17:25](https://ref.ly/Gen17:25) · [GEN 17:26](https://ref.ly/Gen17:26) · [GEN 17:27](https://ref.ly/Gen17:27) · [GEN 21:4](https://ref.ly/Gen21:4) · [GEN 34:15](https://ref.ly/Gen34:15) · [GEN 34:17](https://ref.ly/Gen34:17) · [GEN 34:22](https://ref.ly/Gen34:22) · [GEN 34:22](https://ref.ly/Gen34:22) · [GEN 34:24](https://ref.ly/Gen34:24) · [EXO 12:44](https://ref.ly/Exod12:44) · [EXO 12:48](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48) · [LEV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Lev12:3) · [JOS 5:3](https://ref.ly/Josh5:3) · [JOS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Josh5:4) · [JOS 5:5](https://ref.ly/Josh5:5) · [JOS 5:5](https://ref.ly/Josh5:5) · [JOS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Josh5:7) · [JOS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Josh5:7) · [JOS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Josh5:8) · [JER 9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:24)

Willing \| verb qal, verb nif

**Collocation:** מול אֵת (עָרְלַת) לֵבָב

to circumcise (one's heart) \> to (re)commit oneself (to following God) literally: to remove the foreskin of the heart; hence: \= action by which someone changes his/her attitude towards YHWH, as if by removing something that covers the heart like a foreskin, and so become willing to obey the terms of the covenant and serve YHWH wholeheartedly

[DEU 10:16](https://ref.ly/Deut10:16) · [DEU 30:6](https://ref.ly/Deut30:6) · [JER 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jer4:4)

Dead \| verb hif

to cause to be circumcised exact meaning unknown; probably: \= to kill an uncircumcised enemy, and remove his foreskin as proof

[PSA 118:10](https://ref.ly/Ps118:10)1 · [PSA 118:11](https://ref.ly/Ps118:11)1 · [PSA 118:12](https://ref.ly/Ps118:12)1

1 PSA 118:10 · PSA 118:11 · PSA 118:12 RSV NIV NRSV "to cut off" NJPS NJB "to cut down" GNT "to destroy" \| CEV "to get rid of" \| REB "to drive off"

## מוּל (id: 003780000000000)

מוּל
====

מוּל (H4136)

noun m

Space

in front of, facing, towards \= relational linking an event to a location right in front of another location

[EXO 26:9](https://ref.ly/Exod26:9) · [EXO 28:25](https://ref.ly/Exod28:25) · [EXO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Exod28:27) · [EXO 28:37](https://ref.ly/Exod28:37) · [EXO 34:3](https://ref.ly/Exod34:3) · [EXO 39:18](https://ref.ly/Exod39:18) · [EXO 39:20](https://ref.ly/Exod39:20) · [LEV 5:8](https://ref.ly/Lev5:8)1 · [LEV 8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:9) · [NUM 8:2](https://ref.ly/Num8:2) · [NUM 8:3](https://ref.ly/Num8:3) · [NUM 22:5](https://ref.ly/Num22:5)2 · [DEU 1:1](https://ref.ly/Deut1:1) · [DEU 2:19](https://ref.ly/Deut2:19) · [DEU 3:29](https://ref.ly/Deut3:29) · [DEU 4:46](https://ref.ly/Deut4:46) · [DEU 11:30](https://ref.ly/Deut11:30) · [DEU 34:6](https://ref.ly/Deut34:6) · [JOS 8:33](https://ref.ly/Josh8:33) · [JOS 8:33](https://ref.ly/Josh8:33) · [JOS 9:1](https://ref.ly/Josh9:1) · [JOS 18:18](https://ref.ly/Josh18:18) · [JOS 19:46](https://ref.ly/Josh19:46) · [JOS 22:11](https://ref.ly/Josh22:11) · [1SA 14:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:5) · [1SA 14:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:5) · [1SA 17:30](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:30) · [2SA 5:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:23) · [2SA 11:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:15) · [1KI 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:5) · [1KI 7:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:39) · [1CH 14:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:14) · [2CH 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:10) · [NEH 12:38](https://ref.ly/Neh12:38)3 · [MIC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Mic2:8)4

Connection

(to represent people) before (God) \= relational linking an event to a frame of reference rather than a physical location

[EXO 18:19](https://ref.ly/Exod18:19)

1 LEV 5:8 The way this word is used in this passage shows that the head of the bird is severed at the front but left attached to the rump at the back. 2 NUM 22:5 The reason why מוּל is preceded by מִן in this passage is not exactly clear \| in view of the context the most plausible explanation is that Balak wants to stress the closeness of Israel \| NJB and REB have brought this out clearly by using the expression "at my very door". 3 NEH 12:38 HOTTP\-B NIV NJPS CEV מֹול \| MT לְמֹואל \| RSV NJB NRSV REB GNT "left" \| the interpretation of HOTTP\-B has probably been inspired by the gloss "opposite", which several English translations use to render the word מוּל \| though "opposite" can be used to indicate the relative closeness of two locations (e.g. ), it should not be used to indicate opposite directions, which is outside of the scope of the meaning of this word \| for that reason the interpretation of RSV et al. is to be preferred. 4 MIC 2:8 It is assumed, along with HOTTP\-B that the אֶדֶר represents a piece of clothing that is worn over the שַׂלְמָה \| see for instance TOB "... dessus la tunique, vous enlevez le manteau ..."

Reinier de Blois

Dead \| verb pi, verb hif, verb hof

to cause to die, to kill, to put to death, to finish off \= causative action by which humans, animals, or deities cause (other) humans or animals to stop living

world of the dead \= abode of the dead; ≈ considered to be a remote place, deep in the earth, with power over humankind, without remembrance, without praising God

## מֹותָר (id: 003831000000000)

מֹותָר
======

מֹותָר (H4195)

noun m · יתר

Well

advantage \= something that is of benefit to humans; often used in rhetorical questions suggesting that nothing is of benefit at all in a certain situation

[PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23) · [PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5) · [ECC 3:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:19)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 14:23; Proverbs 21:5; Ecclesiastes 3:19

מִזְבֵּחַ
=========

מִזְבֵּחַ (H4196)

noun m · זבח

Cult Objects

altar \= construction with a flat top, ◄ originally made out of stones that were not cut and shaped with tools, later also out of metal; ► used for burning sacrifices

## מְזוּזָה (id: 003837000000000)

מְזוּזָה
========

מְזוּזָה (H4201)

noun f

Constructions

doorpost \= upright part of a door frame; a few passages mention square door frames and in such cases the horizontal elements are also referred to as doorposts

[EXO 12:7](https://ref.ly/Exod12:7) · [EXO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22) · [EXO 12:23](https://ref.ly/Exod12:23) · [EXO 21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:6) · [DEU 6:9](https://ref.ly/Deut6:9) · [DEU 11:20](https://ref.ly/Deut11:20) · [JDG 16:3](https://ref.ly/Judg16:3) · [1SA 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:9) · [1KI 6:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:31) · [1KI 6:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:33) · [1KI 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:5) · [ISA 57:8](https://ref.ly/Isa57:8) · [EZK 41:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:21) · [EZK 45:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:19) · [EZK 45:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:19) · [EZK 46:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:2)

Constructions

**Collocation:** מְזוּזֹת פֶּתַח

entrance, doorway literally: doorposts of the doorway; hence: \= entrance to a building

[PRO 8:34](https://ref.ly/Prov8:34)

Constructions

**Collocation:** סַף וּמְזוּזָה

threshold and doorpost \> house literally: threshold and doorpost, here used metaphorically to refer to the entire construction: \= building

[EZK 43:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:8) · [EZK 43:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:8)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 12:7; Exodus 12:22; Exodus 12:23; Exodus 21:6; Deuteronomy 6:9; Deuteronomy 11:20; Judges 16:3; 1 Samuel 1:9; 1 Kings 6:31; 1 Kings 6:33; 1 Kings 7:5; Isaiah 57:8; Ezekiel 41:21; Ezekiel 45:19; Ezekiel 46:2; Proverbs 8:34; Ezekiel 43:8

Clothing

Control

## מחה (id: 003865000000000)

מחה
===

מחה (H4229)

verb

Clean \| verb qal

to wipe \= causative action by which humans clean or dry an object or body part by rubbing it with a cloth or with their hands; sometimes extended to refer to God; ≈ sometimes associated with destruction

[2KI 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:13) · [2KI 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:13) · [PRO 30:20](https://ref.ly/Prov30:20) · [ISA 25:8](https://ref.ly/Isa25:8)

Clean \| verb qal, verb nif

to erase, to blot out \= causative action by which humans erase words from parchment or leather, either by scratching away the ink with a sharp implement or by washing it away with water

[EXO 32:32](https://ref.ly/Exod32:32) · [EXO 32:33](https://ref.ly/Exod32:33) · [NUM 5:23](https://ref.ly/Num5:23) · [PSA 69:29](https://ref.ly/Ps69:29)

Non\-Know \| verb qal, verb hif, verb nif

to blot out, to wipe out \= causative action by which deities or humans ensure that (other) humans and/or their actions will no longer be remembered

[EXO 17:14](https://ref.ly/Exod17:14) · [EXO 17:14](https://ref.ly/Exod17:14) · [DEU 9:14](https://ref.ly/Deut9:14) · [DEU 25:6](https://ref.ly/Deut25:6) · [DEU 25:19](https://ref.ly/Deut25:19) · [DEU 29:19](https://ref.ly/Deut29:19) · [2KI 14:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:27) · [NEH 13:14](https://ref.ly/Neh13:14) · [PSA 9:6](https://ref.ly/Ps9:6) · [PSA 109:13](https://ref.ly/Ps109:13) · [PRO 6:33](https://ref.ly/Prov6:33)

Innocent \| verb qal, verb hif, verb nif

to wipe away, to forgive \= causative action by which deities decide to no longer hold a certain human's behavior against that person, as if by erasing that sin from one's record

[NEH 3:37](https://ref.ly/Neh3:37) · [PSA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Ps51:3) · [PSA 51:11](https://ref.ly/Ps51:11) · [PSA 109:14](https://ref.ly/Ps109:14) · [ISA 43:25](https://ref.ly/Isa43:25) · [ISA 44:22](https://ref.ly/Isa44:22) · [JER 18:23](https://ref.ly/Jer18:23)

Non\-Exist \| verb qal, verb hif, verb nif

to blot out, to wipe out, to destroy \= causative action by which humans or deities totally remove (other) humans and objects from existence without leaving a trace

[GEN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Gen6:7) · [GEN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Gen7:4) · [GEN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Gen7:23) · [GEN 7:23](https://ref.ly/Gen7:23) · [JDG 21:17](https://ref.ly/Judg21:17) · [2KI 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:13) · [PRO 31:3](https://ref.ly/Prov31:3) · [EZK 6:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:6)

verb

Space \| verb qal

to reach \= state in which a geographical boundary reaches a certain place before taking a turn

[NUM 34:11](https://ref.ly/Num34:11)

verb

Contain \| verb pi ptc.act

full of marrow \= state in which a dish contains a large quantity of marrow, ≈ which is considered to be a delicacy

[ISA 25:6](https://ref.ly/Isa25:6)

Reinier de Blois

## מחץ (id: 003905000000000)

מחץ
===

מחץ (H4272)

verb · מַחַץ

Impact \| verb qal \| ≈ מחק \| ≠ רפא

to strike, to smash, to crush \= action by which humans strike other humans or animals with a hammer, club or similar object, resulting in breaking the underlying bones

[NUM 24:17](https://ref.ly/Num24:17) · [DEU 32:39](https://ref.ly/Deut32:39) · [DEU 33:11](https://ref.ly/Deut33:11) · [JDG 5:26](https://ref.ly/Judg5:26) · [2SA 22:39](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:39) · [JOB 5:18](https://ref.ly/Job5:18) · [JOB 26:12](https://ref.ly/Job26:12) · [PSA 18:39](https://ref.ly/Ps18:39) · [PSA 68:22](https://ref.ly/Ps68:22) · [HAB 3:13](https://ref.ly/Hab3:13)

Impact \| verb qal

to smash, to break exact meaning unsure, probably: \= action by which humans hit objects with a hammer, club or similar object, resulting in breaking them into multiple pieces

[NUM 24:8](https://ref.ly/Num24:8)1

Control \| verb qal

to defeat totally \= causative action by which an army gains the upper hand over an opposing army with a total victory

[PSA 110:5](https://ref.ly/Ps110:5) · [PSA 110:6](https://ref.ly/Ps110:6)

Stance \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מחץ רֶגֶל

to plunge (one's) foot literally: to strike (one's) foot (in a substance); hence: \= action by which humans put their foot down with some force

[PSA 68:24](https://ref.ly/Ps68:24)

1 NUM 24:8 NJPS GNT "smash (arrows)" NRSV "strike (with arrows)" REB "break" CEV "crushing" \| RSV NIV NJB ESV "pierce (with arrows)"

Reinier de Blois

## מַחֲרֵשָׁה (id: 003914000000000)

מַחֲרֵשָׁה
==========

מַחֲרֵשָׁה (H4281 · H4282) — מַחֲרֶשֶׁת [SAHD](https://sahd-online.com/words/machareshah/)

noun f · חרשׁ

Tools \| ≈ אֵת · קַרְדֹּם

plough(\-point) \= triangular blade ◄ made of metal (bronze or iron), and part of a larger contraption ► used for breaking the soil to make it ready for cultivation

[1SA 13:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:20) · [1SA 13:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:20)1 · [1SA 13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:21)

1 1SA 13:20 MT HOTTP\-B NJPS מַחֲרֵשָׁתֹו \| LXX RSV NIV NJB REB NRSV REB GNT CEV חרמשׂו \= חֶרְמֵשׂ "sickle, scythe". 'Ploughshare' is the traditional translation, but not adequate. Triangular blade does seem to be correct. Options to handle the (odd) repetition of 'plougpoint' in 1SAM13:20\. (1\) Emendation cf. versiones; (2\) Follow vocalisation Masoretes, imlpying different object (3\) Or part of the totality of plough.

Paul Sanders

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 13:20; 1 Samuel 13:21

## מטא (id: 003923000000000)

מטא
===

מטא (A4291)

verb

Space \| verb pe'al

to reach \= a process by which humans or plants stop moving or growing because they have reached a certain place or position

[DAN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Dan4:8) · [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17) · [DAN 6:25](https://ref.ly/Dan6:25) · [DAN 7:13](https://ref.ly/Dan7:13)

Engage \| verb pe'al

to come upon, to happen literally: to reach; hence: \= a process by which a certain condition or event starts affecting someone

[DAN 4:21](https://ref.ly/Dan4:21) · [DAN 4:25](https://ref.ly/Dan4:25)

Intense \| verb pe'al

to reach literally: to reach (a place); hence: \= a process by which a condition stops increasing in intensity because it has reached a certain level

[DAN 4:19](https://ref.ly/Dan4:19)

Time \| verb pe'al

(time) came, (time) arrived literally: (time) reaches; hence: \= the moment when, after a certain amount of time, a particular event has taken place

[DAN 7:22](https://ref.ly/Dan7:22)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Daniel 4:8; Daniel 4:17; Daniel 6:25; Daniel 7:13; Daniel 4:21; Daniel 4:25; Daniel 4:19; Daniel 7:22

## מַטָּה (id: 003928000000000)

מַטָּה
======

מַטָּה (H4295)

noun m · נטה

Spatial Referents

beneath, down \= a referent pointing to the lower part of an object or area

[EXO 26:24](https://ref.ly/Exod26:24) · [EXO 27:5](https://ref.ly/Exod27:5) · [EXO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Exod28:27) · [EXO 36:29](https://ref.ly/Exod36:29) · [EXO 38:4](https://ref.ly/Exod38:4) · [EXO 39:20](https://ref.ly/Exod39:20) · [2KI 19:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:30) · [2CH 32:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:30) · [PRO 15:24](https://ref.ly/Prov15:24) · [ECC 3:21](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:21) · [ISA 37:31](https://ref.ly/Isa37:31) · [JER 31:37](https://ref.ly/Jer31:37) · [EZK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:27) · [EZK 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:2)

Insignificant

lower (in status) literally: lower; hence: \= state in which a person has a relatively low status within a community

[DEU 28:13](https://ref.ly/Deut28:13) · [DEU 28:43](https://ref.ly/Deut28:43) · [DEU 28:43](https://ref.ly/Deut28:43)

Lax

less literally: lower; hence: \= state in which an event takes place with a relatively low intensity

[EZR 9:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:13)

Young

below (in years) \= state in which humans have a relatively low age

[1CH 27:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:23)

courtyard of the guard literally: court of the guard; hence: \= a place in the royal palace ► where certain prisoners were confined

Names of Constructions

**Collocation:** שַׁעַר מַטָּרָה

Gate of the Guard \= one of the city gates of Jerusalem

[NEH 12:39](https://ref.ly/Neh12:39)

noun f

Objects

target \= a person, object, or place selected as the aim of a bowshot

## מַטְרִי (id: 003941000000000)

מַטְרִי
=======

מַטְרִי (H4309)

name

Names of Groups

Matrite \= clan of king Saul; ◄ tribe of Benjamin

## מִי (id: 003942000000000)

מִי
===

מִי (H4310)

inter.pron

Object Referents

who? \= a reference to an unknown person or deity; normally used with the purpose of identifying that person or deity; sometimes used in rhetorical questions expressing one's conviction that that person or deity cannot be identified

## מִיכָל (id: 003956000000000)

מִיכָל
======

מִיכָל (H4323)

noun m

Waterbodies

**Collocation:** מִיכַל מַיִם

pool, stream \= a body of water of unknown shape or size

[2SA 17:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:20)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 17:20


## מַיִם (id: 003957000000000)

מַיִם
=====

מַיִם (H4325)

noun m · אָבֵל מָיִם

Liquids \| Waterbodies

water \= water as the indispensible liquid on which the existence of all life depends, used for drinking, preparing food, washing, irrigating, etc.; found under the earth, on the earth, and above the earth; sometimes used as a euphemism to denote certain body liquids; ≈ associated with abundance and refreshment, but also with instability, and danger, etc.

name

Names of People

Mijamin \= man; ◄ tribe of Levi; ► family head in charge of a division of priests; time of David

Names of People

Mijamin \= man; ◄ descendant of Parosh; time of Ezra

[EZR 10:25](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:25)

Mijamin \= various men; ► priests; tribe of Levi; time of Zerubbabel

## מִיץ (id: 003961000000000)

מִיץ
====

מִיץ (H4330)

noun m · מֵץ

Impact

pressure \= causative action by which humans put pressure on milk by agitating it in a churn in order to produce curds

[PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33)1

Impact

pressure \= causative action by which humans put pressure on the body of other humans, e.g. by wringing, squeezing, or pinching it

[PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33)1

Intense

pressure \= causative action by which humans interact with people in such a way that it intensifies certain emotions, ◄ compared to the churning of milk

[PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33)1

1 PRO 30:33 · PRO 30:33 · PRO 30:33 Note that the same word is used with three different senses in one single verse.

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 30:33

Traps \| ≈ חַכָּה · חֵרֶם

casting net \= circular net with weights and a draw rope around its edge of roughly 6–7\.5 meters in diameter; ► thrown from a boat or the shore in a circular motion so that it lands with the net fully spread out on the water; the weights carry it down, trapping fish underneath; then the fisherman uses the draw rope to close the net and pull it back to himself; ≈ associated with aggression and control

Non\-Possess \| verb nif, verb hit

to sell oneself \= action by which humans hand themselves over to other humans to become their slaves

[LEV 25:39](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39) · [LEV 25:47](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47) · [LEV 25:50](https://ref.ly/Lev25:50) · [DEU 28:68](https://ref.ly/Deut28:68)

Control \| verb qal

to surrender literally: to sell; hence: \= causative action by which deities surrender their people to be controled by others

[DEU 32:30](https://ref.ly/Deut32:30) · [JDG 2:14](https://ref.ly/Judg2:14) · [JDG 3:8](https://ref.ly/Judg3:8) · [JDG 4:2](https://ref.ly/Judg4:2) · [JDG 4:9](https://ref.ly/Judg4:9) · [JDG 10:7](https://ref.ly/Judg10:7) · [1SA 12:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:9) · [EZK 30:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:12)

Engage \| verb hit

to sell oneself (to an activity), to engage oneself consciously literally: to sell oneself; hence: \= action by which humans make a conscious decision to engage in a certain type of behavior; ≈ only used in the context of morally and ethically unacceptable behavior

[1KI 21:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:20) · [1KI 21:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:25) · [2KI 17:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:17)

Non\-Engage \| ≠ קנה

to sell, to part with (a quality) literally: to sell (a quality); hence: \= action by which humans part with a certain quality

Engage \| verb qal

**Collocation:** יָמִין מלא

[PSA 48:11](https://ref.ly/Ps48:11)

Engage \| verb qal

**Collocation:** לֵב מָלֵא

[ECC 9:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:3)

Engage \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מָתְנַיִם מלא

to be filled \> to be engaged literally: (one's) loins are filled (with an experience); hence: \= state in which the entire human body is affected by a certain emotion

[ISA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Isa21:3)

Engage \| verb qal, verb nif

**Collocation:** פֶּה מלא · שְׂפָתַיִם מלא

to be filled \> to be engaged literally: (one's) mouth or lips are filled (with an activity); hence: \= state in which humans are fully engaged in the communication of certain information

[PSA 10:7](https://ref.ly/Ps10:7) · [PSA 71:8](https://ref.ly/Ps71:8) · [PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2) · [ISA 30:27](https://ref.ly/Isa30:27)

Engage \| verb qal, verb nif, verb hitpe'el

to be filled \> to be engaged literally: to be filled (with an activity); hence: \= state in which humans are fully engaged in a certain activity, either as victim or as active participant

[DEU 33:23](https://ref.ly/Deut33:23) · [DEU 34:9](https://ref.ly/Deut34:9) · [1KI 7:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:14) · [EST 3:5](https://ref.ly/Esth3:5) · [EST 5:9](https://ref.ly/Esth5:9) · [JOB 32:18](https://ref.ly/Job32:18) · [JOB 36:17](https://ref.ly/Job36:17) · [PRO 12:21](https://ref.ly/Prov12:21) · [ISA 51:20](https://ref.ly/Isa51:20) · [JER 6:11](https://ref.ly/Jer6:11) · [EZK 23:33](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:33) · [DAN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Dan3:19) · [MIC 3:8](https://ref.ly/Mic3:8) · [MIC 6:12](https://ref.ly/Mic6:12)

Engage \| verb pi

to fill \> to engage literally: to fill (with an activity); hence: \= causative action in which deities cause humans to be fully engaged in a certain activity, either as victim or as active participant

[EXO 35:35](https://ref.ly/Exod35:35) · [JOB 8:21](https://ref.ly/Job8:21) · [ISA 33:5](https://ref.ly/Isa33:5) · [JER 13:13](https://ref.ly/Jer13:13) · [JER 15:17](https://ref.ly/Jer15:17)

Happen \| verb qal, verb nif

to be full of \= state or process by which a location is dominated by a certain event, to the extent that it dominates everything else, ◄ as if it were completely filled with that event

[GEN 6:11](https://ref.ly/Gen6:11) · [GEN 6:13](https://ref.ly/Gen6:13) · [LEV 19:29](https://ref.ly/Lev19:29) · [NUM 14:21](https://ref.ly/Num14:21) · [EZR 9:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:11) · [JOB 20:11](https://ref.ly/Job20:11) · [PSA 33:5](https://ref.ly/Ps33:5) · [PSA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Ps38:8) · [PSA 72:19](https://ref.ly/Ps72:19) · [PSA 74:20](https://ref.ly/Ps74:20) · [PSA 119:64](https://ref.ly/Ps119:64) · [ISA 11:9](https://ref.ly/Isa11:9) · [ISA 22:2](https://ref.ly/Isa22:2) · [JER 5:27](https://ref.ly/Jer5:27) · [JER 46:12](https://ref.ly/Jer46:12) · [JER 51:5](https://ref.ly/Jer51:5) · [EZK 7:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:23) · [EZK 7:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:23) · [EZK 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:9) · [EZK 28:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:16) · [NAM 3:1](https://ref.ly/Nah3:1) · [HAB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hab2:14) · [HAB 3:3](https://ref.ly/Hab3:3)

Happen \| verb qal

to fill with \= causative action or process by which deities cause a location to be dominated by a certain activity, to the extent that it dominates everything else, ◄ as if it were completely filled with that event

[JER 16:18](https://ref.ly/Jer16:18) · [EZK 8:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:17) · [ZEP 1:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:9) · [HAG 2:7](https://ref.ly/Hag2:7)

Happen \| verb pi

to fulfill literally: to fill (words or plans); hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause plans or promises to be carried out as intended

[1KI 2:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:27) · [1KI 8:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:15) · [1KI 8:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:24) · [2CH 6:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:4) · [2CH 6:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:15) · [2CH 36:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:21) · [PSA 20:5](https://ref.ly/Ps20:5) · [PSA 20:6](https://ref.ly/Ps20:6) · [JER 44:25](https://ref.ly/Jer44:25)

Intense \| verb qal

to be full \= state in which an activity is achieved to a full extent

[JOB 20:22](https://ref.ly/Job20:22) · [JER 4:5](https://ref.ly/Jer4:5)

Non\-Possess \| verb pi

to give in full literally: to fill (something to someone); hence: \= causative action by which humans give a full number of objects to someone else

[1SA 18:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:27)

Relief \| verb nif, verb qal \| ≈ שׂבע

to be satisfied \= process by which someone's desire finds relief

[EXO 15:9](https://ref.ly/Exod15:9) · [ECC 1:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:8) · [ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7)

Relief \| verb pi

to satisfy literally: to fill; hence: \= action by which humans do what is needed to find relief from their desire

[PRO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30)

Relief \| verb pi \| ≈ רוה · שׂבע

to satisfy literally: to fill; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to find relief from their desire

[JOB 38:39](https://ref.ly/Job38:39) · [PSA 107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:9) · [JER 31:25](https://ref.ly/Jer31:25)

Role \| verb pi, verb qal

**Collocation:** מלא יָד

to consecrate oneself literally: to fill (one's own) hands; hence: \= action by which humans do what is needed to prepare themselves to serve a deity, either in a sanctuary or in a more general sense

[EXO 32:29](https://ref.ly/Exod32:29) · [LEV 21:10](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10) · [1CH 29:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:5) · [2CH 13:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:9) · [2CH 29:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:31)

Role \| verb pi

**Collocation:** מלא יָד

to ordain, to install in office, to consecrate literally: to fill (someone else's) hands; hence: \= causative action by which humans do what is needed to prepare (other) humans or objects for the service of a deity

[EXO 28:41](https://ref.ly/Exod28:41) · [EXO 29:9](https://ref.ly/Exod29:9) · [EXO 29:29](https://ref.ly/Exod29:29) · [EXO 29:33](https://ref.ly/Exod29:33) · [EXO 29:35](https://ref.ly/Exod29:35) · [LEV 8:33](https://ref.ly/Lev8:33) · [LEV 16:32](https://ref.ly/Lev16:32) · [NUM 3:3](https://ref.ly/Num3:3) · [JDG 17:5](https://ref.ly/Judg17:5) · [JDG 17:12](https://ref.ly/Judg17:12) · [1KI 13:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:33) · [EZK 43:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:26)

Shame \| verb pi

**Collocation:** מלא פָּנֶה קָלוֹן

to heap shame (on someone) literally: to fill (someone's) face with shame; hence: \= causative action by which deities cause humans to be deeply ashamed in such a way that it shows on their faces

[PSA 83:17](https://ref.ly/Ps83:17)

Space \| verb pi, verb pu'al

to set literally: to fill; hence: \= causative action by which a craftsman attaches a mounted stone on a certain surface

[EXO 28:17](https://ref.ly/Exod28:17) · [EXO 31:5](https://ref.ly/Exod31:5) · [EXO 35:33](https://ref.ly/Exod35:33) · [EXO 39:10](https://ref.ly/Exod39:10) · [SNG 5:14](https://ref.ly/Song5:14)

Space \| verb pi

to put on the string literally: to fill; hence: \= action by which humans put an arrow on the string of a bow in order to shoot it

[ZEC 9:13](https://ref.ly/Zech9:13)

Speak \| verb pi

**Collocation:** מלא פֶּה

\= to speak much literally: to fill (one's) mouth (with arguments); hence: \= action by which humans are actively engaged in speaking

[JOB 23:4](https://ref.ly/Job23:4)

Stance \| verb pi

**Collocation:** מלא יָד בְּקֶשֶׁת

to draw the bow literally: to fill (one's) hand on the bow; hence: \= action by which someone draws his bow in order to shoot an arrow

[2KI 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:24)

Think \| verb qal

**Collocation:** לֵב מלא

to be determined literally: (one's) heart is filled (to do something); hence: \= state in which someone is fully determined to carry out a certain activity

[EST 7:5](https://ref.ly/Esth7:5) · [ECC 8:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:11)

True \| verb pi

to confirm literally: to fill (someone's words); hence: \= causative action by which humans confirm that certain things are true

[1KI 1:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:14)

Willing \| verb pi

**Collocation:** מלא אַחֲרֵי

to be fully behind someone literally: they fill behind (someone); hence: \= state in which humans are wholeheartedly in support of someone else

[NUM 14:24](https://ref.ly/Num14:24) · [NUM 32:11](https://ref.ly/Num32:11) · [NUM 32:12](https://ref.ly/Num32:12) · [DEU 1:36](https://ref.ly/Deut1:36) · [JOS 14:8](https://ref.ly/Josh14:8) · [JOS 14:9](https://ref.ly/Josh14:9) · [JOS 14:14](https://ref.ly/Josh14:14) · [1KI 11:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:6)

## מָלֵא (id: 004018000000000)

מָלֵא
=====

מָלֵא (H4392)

adj · מלא

Contain

filled, full \= state in which a certain location, container, or surface is filled or covered with so many objects that little or no space is left

[GEN 41:7](https://ref.ly/Gen41:7) · [GEN 41:22](https://ref.ly/Gen41:22) · [2KI 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:4) · [PSA 144:13](https://ref.ly/Ps144:13) · [ECC 1:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:7) · [EZK 17:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:3) · [EZK 36:38](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:38) · [AMO 2:13](https://ref.ly/Amos2:13)

Complete

full literally: full; hence: \= state in which a particular process has come to a full end, without having been cut short

[PSA 75:9](https://ref.ly/Ps75:9) · [NAM 1:10](https://ref.ly/Nah1:10)

Complete

**Collocation:** כֶּסֶף מָלֵא

full literally: full silver; hence: \= state in which the amount paid in a transaction is the full price, without any discount

[GEN 23:9](https://ref.ly/Gen23:9) · [1CH 21:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:22) · [1CH 21:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:24)

Happen

full (of an activity) \= state in which a location is dominated by a certain quality or activity, to the extent that it dominates everything else, ◄ as if it were completely filled with that quality of activity

[ISA 1:21](https://ref.ly/Isa1:21) · [EZK 28:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:12)

Intense

full, intense, strong literally: full; hence: \= process by which a certain activity take place with maximum intensity

[JER 4:12](https://ref.ly/Jer4:12) · [JER 12:6](https://ref.ly/Jer12:6)

Old

**Collocation:** מְלֵא יָמִים

aged literally: full of days; hence: \= state in which humans have reached a very advanced age

[JER 6:11](https://ref.ly/Jer6:11)

Pregnant

pregnant, with child literally: filled; hence: \= state in which a woman is with child

[ECC 11:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:5)

Rich \| ≠ רֵיקָם

rich literally: filled; hence: \= state in which humans have ample resources

[RUT 1:21](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:21)

1 PSA 73:10 RSV NRSV ESV "and find no fault in them" REB "and find in them nothing blameworthy"

## מְלָאכָה (id: 004024000000000)

מְלָאכָה
========

מְלָאכָה (H4399) — מְלֶאכֶת

noun f · מַלְאָךְ · מַלְאָכוּת

Serve

work, task, craft, service \= an activity involving a physical effort and usually requiring a certain amount of training or skill

noun m · עִיר־הַמֶּלַח

Minerals \| ≈ גָּפְרִית

salt \= white crystalline substance, mainly consisting of sodium chloride; ► normally used for seasoning or preserving food; ≈ associated with infertility of the soil but also with permanence

[GEN 19:26](https://ref.ly/Gen19:26) · [LEV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lev2:13) · [LEV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lev2:13) · [LEV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lev2:13) · [NUM 18:19](https://ref.ly/Num18:19) · [DEU 29:22](https://ref.ly/Deut29:22) · [JDG 9:45](https://ref.ly/Judg9:45) · [2KI 2:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:20) · [2KI 2:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:21) · [2CH 13:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:5) · [JOB 6:6](https://ref.ly/Job6:6) · [EZK 43:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:24) · [EZK 47:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:11) · [ZEP 2:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:9)

Names of Landforms

**Collocation:** גֵּיא מֶלַח

Valley of Salt \= valley, ◄ located south of Dead Sea; ► location where several battles with the Edomites took place

[2KI 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:7)

Names of Waterbodies

**Collocation:** יָם מֶלֶח

Salt Sea \= literally: Sea of Salt; one of the names of the Dead Sea, because of the high concentration of salt in its water

[GEN 14:3](https://ref.ly/Gen14:3) · [NUM 34:3](https://ref.ly/Num34:3) · [NUM 34:12](https://ref.ly/Num34:12) · [DEU 3:17](https://ref.ly/Deut3:17) · [JOS 3:16](https://ref.ly/Josh3:16) · [JOS 12:3](https://ref.ly/Josh12:3) · [JOS 15:2](https://ref.ly/Josh15:2) · [JOS 15:5](https://ref.ly/Josh15:5) · [JOS 18:19](https://ref.ly/Josh18:19)

noun m · מלח

Clothing \| noun m pl \| ≈ סְחָבָה

old clothes, rags \= pieces of worn out clothing that are no longer fit for wearing

[JER 38:11](https://ref.ly/Jer38:11) · [JER 38:12](https://ref.ly/Jer38:12)

1 2KI 14:7 MT\-K המלח \| MT\-Q מֶלַח

## מְלֵחָה (id: 004042000000000)

מְלֵחָה
=======

מְלֵחָה (H4420)

noun f · מלח

Landscapes

salt land, salt waste \= land of which the soil contains a relatively high quantity of salt and other minerals that render it infertile

[JOB 39:6](https://ref.ly/Job39:6) · [PSA 107:34](https://ref.ly/Ps107:34) · [JER 17:6](https://ref.ly/Jer17:6)

Arrange \| verb qal

**Collocation:** ערך מִלְחָמָה

Soldiers

**Collocation:** אִישׁ מִלְחָמֹות

warrior literally: person of battles; \= person specialized and experienced in warfare; ≈ associated with strength and bravery

Soldiers

**Collocation:** גִּבֹּור מִלְחָמָה

valiant man of war literally: a hero of battle; hence: \= a valiant soldier

[2CH 13:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:3) · [PSA 24:8](https://ref.ly/Ps24:8)

Soldiers

**Collocation:** עַם מִלְחָמָה

troops literally: a people of war; hence: \= a group of soldiers

[JOS 8:1](https://ref.ly/Josh8:1) · [JOS 8:3](https://ref.ly/Josh8:3) · [JOS 8:11](https://ref.ly/Josh8:11) · [JOS 10:7](https://ref.ly/Josh10:7) · [JOS 11:7](https://ref.ly/Josh11:7) · [2SA 11:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:7)

Soldiers

**Collocation:** שַׂר מִלְחָמָה

battle officer literally: an officer of battle; hence: \= a soldier in charge of other soldiers with the responsibility of leading them in battle

[2CH 32:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:6)

Weapons

**Collocation:** כְּלִי מִלְחָמָה

weapons literally: tool for battle; hence: \= implement ► designed to be used in battle, for inflicting physical damage on others, or for protecting oneself against aggression from others

## מלט (id: 004044000000000)

מלט
===

מלט (H4422)

verb

Flee \| verb nif \| ≈ נוס

to escape \= action by which humans move away as fast as possible from a place that is considered insecure ► in order to save their lives

[GEN 19:17](https://ref.ly/Gen19:17) · [GEN 19:17](https://ref.ly/Gen19:17) · [GEN 19:19](https://ref.ly/Gen19:19) · [GEN 19:20](https://ref.ly/Gen19:20) · [GEN 19:22](https://ref.ly/Gen19:22) · [JDG 3:26](https://ref.ly/Judg3:26) · [JDG 3:26](https://ref.ly/Judg3:26) · [JDG 3:29](https://ref.ly/Judg3:29) · [1SA 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:10) · [1SA 19:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:12) · [1SA 19:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:17) · [1SA 19:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:18) · [1SA 22:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:1) · [1SA 22:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:20) · [1SA 23:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:13) · [1SA 27:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:1) · [1SA 27:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:1) · [1SA 27:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:1) · [1SA 30:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:17) · [2SA 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:3) · [2SA 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:6) · [1KI 18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:40) · [1KI 19:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:17) · [1KI 19:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:17) · [1KI 20:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:20) · [2KI 10:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:24)1 · [2KI 19:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:37) · [2CH 16:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:7) · [JOB 1:15](https://ref.ly/Job1:15) · [JOB 1:16](https://ref.ly/Job1:16) · [JOB 1:17](https://ref.ly/Job1:17) · [JOB 1:19](https://ref.ly/Job1:19) · [PSA 124:7](https://ref.ly/Ps124:7) · [PSA 124:7](https://ref.ly/Ps124:7) · [ISA 37:38](https://ref.ly/Isa37:38) · [JER 41:15](https://ref.ly/Jer41:15) · [JER 46:6](https://ref.ly/Jer46:6) · [JER 48:19](https://ref.ly/Jer48:19) · [AMO 9:1](https://ref.ly/Amos9:1) · [ZEC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Zech2:11)

Move \| verb hit

to escape, to shoot out literally: to escape; hence: \= process by which objects come out of a certain location, possibly with some speed or force

[JOB 41:11](https://ref.ly/Job41:11)

Move \| verb nif

to slip away literally: to escape; hence: \= action by which humans leave a certain location unnoticed

[1SA 20:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:29)

Birth \| verb pi

to lay (an egg) literally: to cause to escape; hence: \= causative action by which birds produce an egg from inside the body

[ISA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Isa34:15)

Birth \| verb hif \| ≈ ילד · פלט

to bring forth literally: to cause to escape; hence: \= causative action by which human females give birth to offspring

[ISA 66:7](https://ref.ly/Isa66:7)

Safe \| verb nif, verb hit \| ≈ ישׁע · פְּלֵיטָה

to escape, to be delivered, to go free literally: to escape; hence: \= process by which humans are able to elude danger or suffering

[EST 4:13](https://ref.ly/Esth4:13) · [JOB 19:20](https://ref.ly/Job19:20) · [JOB 22:30](https://ref.ly/Job22:30) · [PSA 22:6](https://ref.ly/Ps22:6) · [PRO 11:21](https://ref.ly/Prov11:21) · [PRO 19:5](https://ref.ly/Prov19:5) · [PRO 28:26](https://ref.ly/Prov28:26) · [ECC 7:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:26) · [ISA 20:6](https://ref.ly/Isa20:6) · [ISA 49:24](https://ref.ly/Isa49:24) · [ISA 49:25](https://ref.ly/Isa49:25) · [JER 32:4](https://ref.ly/Jer32:4) · [JER 34:3](https://ref.ly/Jer34:3) · [JER 38:18](https://ref.ly/Jer38:18) · [JER 38:23](https://ref.ly/Jer38:23) · [JER 48:8](https://ref.ly/Jer48:8) · [EZK 17:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:15) · [EZK 17:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:15) · [EZK 17:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:18) · [DAN 11:41](https://ref.ly/Dan11:41) · [DAN 12:1](https://ref.ly/Dan12:1) · [JOL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Joel3:5) · [MAL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Mal3:15)

Safe \| verb pi, verb hif \| ≈ גנן · נצל · פדה · פסח · תְּשׁוּעָה

to deliver, to rescue, to save literally: to cause to escape; hence: \= causative process by which humans, deities or animals cause humans to elude danger or suffering

[2SA 19:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:10) · [2KI 23:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:18) · [JOB 6:23](https://ref.ly/Job6:23) · [JOB 20:20](https://ref.ly/Job20:20) · [JOB 22:30](https://ref.ly/Job22:30) · [JOB 29:12](https://ref.ly/Job29:12) · [PSA 33:17](https://ref.ly/Ps33:17)2 · [PSA 41:2](https://ref.ly/Ps41:2) · [PSA 107:20](https://ref.ly/Ps107:20) · [ECC 8:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:8) · [ECC 9:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:15) · [ISA 31:5](https://ref.ly/Isa31:5) · [ISA 46:2](https://ref.ly/Isa46:2) · [ISA 46:4](https://ref.ly/Isa46:4) · [JER 39:18](https://ref.ly/Jer39:18) · [JER 39:18](https://ref.ly/Jer39:18)

Safe \| verb pi

**Collocation:** מלט נֶפֶשׁ

to save (someone's) life literally: to cause someone's self to escape; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities save themselves or others from death

[1SA 19:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:11) · [2SA 19:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:6) · [1KI 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:12) · [PSA 89:49](https://ref.ly/Ps89:49) · [PSA 116:4](https://ref.ly/Ps116:4) · [JER 48:6](https://ref.ly/Jer48:6) · [JER 51:6](https://ref.ly/Jer51:6) · [JER 51:45](https://ref.ly/Jer51:45) · [EZK 33:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:5) · [AMO 2:14](https://ref.ly/Amos2:14) · [AMO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Amos2:15)3 · [AMO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Amos2:15)

1 2KI 10:24 Most commentators have argued that the verb should be read as a piel instead of a niphal 2 PSA 33:17 Although the passive of σῴζω in the LXX implies the reading of
a niph (יִמָּלֵט) cf. Tg) rather than the piel, the verb may not require revocalisation. There may be an implied indefinite object, and the Peshitta does include an object in its translation (see piel Versions, Peshitta). 3 AMO 2:15 The passive of διασῴζω in the LXX implies the reading of the
niphal (יִמָּלֵט) cf. Vg, Tg, Pesh), but this may be a characteristic alteration by the Versions to assist the sense. The piel, if retained, would have to be interpreted as intransitive, or to share the same object נפשׁו with the verb in the next line.

J.K. Aitken

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 19:17; Genesis 19:19; Genesis 19:20; Genesis 19:22; Judges 3:26; Judges 3:29; 1 Samuel 19:10; 1 Samuel 19:12; 1 Samuel 19:17; 1 Samuel 19:18; 1 Samuel 22:1; 1 Samuel 22:20; 1 Samuel 23:13; 1 Samuel 27:1; 1 Samuel 30:17; 2 Samuel 1:3; 2 Samuel 4:6; 1 Kings 18:40; 1 Kings 19:17; 1 Kings 20:20; 2 Kings 10:24; 2 Kings 19:37; 2 Chronicles 16:7; Job 1:15; Job 1:16; Job 1:17; Job 1:19; Psalms 124:7; Isaiah 37:38; Jeremiah 41:15; Jeremiah 46:6; Jeremiah 48:19; Amos 9:1; Zechariah 2:11; Job 41:11; 1 Samuel 20:29; Isaiah 34:15; Isaiah 66:7; Esther 4:13; Job 19:20; Job 22:30; Psalms 22:6; Proverbs 11:21; Proverbs 19:5; Proverbs 28:26; Ecclesiastes 7:26; Isaiah 20:6; Isaiah 49:24; Isaiah 49:25; Jeremiah 32:4; Jeremiah 34:3; Jeremiah 38:18; Jeremiah 38:23; Jeremiah 48:8; Ezekiel 17:15; Ezekiel 17:18; Daniel 11:41; Daniel 12:1; Joel 3:5; Malachi 3:15; 2 Samuel 19:10; 2 Kings 23:18; Job 6:23; Job 20:20; Job 29:12; Psalms 33:17; Psalms 41:2; Psalms 107:20; Ecclesiastes 8:8; Ecclesiastes 9:15; Isaiah 31:5; Isaiah 46:2; Isaiah 46:4; Jeremiah 39:18; 1 Samuel 19:11; 2 Samuel 19:6; 1 Kings 1:12; Psalms 89:49; Psalms 116:4; Jeremiah 48:6; Jeremiah 51:6; Jeremiah 51:45; Ezekiel 33:5; Amos 2:14; Amos 2:15

Think \| verb nif

## מְלַךְ (id: 004051000000000)

מְלַךְ
======

מְלַךְ (H4431)

noun m

Know

advice, counsel \= causative action by which humans or deities offer guidance and recommendations to humans to help them take the right decisions

[DAN 4:24](https://ref.ly/Dan4:24)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Daniel 4:24


## מֶלֶךְ (id: 004052000000000)

מֶלֶךְ
======

מֶלֶךְ (H4428 · H4429 · A4430) [SAHD](SAHD@http://www.sahd.div.ed.ac.uk/_media/lexeme:pdf:hae-melek-salvesen_a-oxf-1999.pdf)

noun m · מלך · אֲבִימֶלֶךְ · אֱלִימֶלֶךְ · מַלְכִּיאֵל · מַלְכִּיָּה · מַלְכִּיָּהוּ · מַלְכִּי־צֶדֶק · מַלְכִּירָם · מַלְכִּי־שׁוּעַ

Leaders

king, ruler \= supreme secular and male ruler of a nation or people; ≈ associated with authority, power, honor, but also with justice; also used as a reference to one's deity

Names of Constructions

**Collocation:** שַׁעַר מֶלֶךְ

King's Gate \= one of the gates of the temple in Jerusalem on the east side

[1CH 9:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:18)

name

Names of People

Melech \= man; ◄ son of Micah; tribe of Benjamin

[1CH 8:35](https://ref.ly/1Chr8:35) · [1CH 9:41](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:41)

Names of Deities

Melech, Molech \= deity; ► worshipped by Canaanites; usually vocalized as מֹלֶךְ

## מַלְכָּה (id: 004056000000000)

מַלְכָּה
========

מַלְכָּה (A4433 · H4436) [SAHD](SADH@http://www.sahd.div.ed.ac.uk/_media/lexeme:pdf:ham-malka-salvesen_a-oxf-1999.pdf)

noun f · מלך

Leaders

queen \= female, foreign ruler of a nation or area

[1KI 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:1) · [1KI 10:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:4) · [1KI 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:10) · [1KI 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:13) · [2CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1) · [2CH 9:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:3) · [2CH 9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:9) · [2CH 9:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:12) · [EST 1:9](https://ref.ly/Esth1:9) · [EST 1:11](https://ref.ly/Esth1:11) · [EST 1:12](https://ref.ly/Esth1:12) · [EST 1:15](https://ref.ly/Esth1:15) · [EST 1:16](https://ref.ly/Esth1:16) · [EST 1:17](https://ref.ly/Esth1:17) · [EST 1:17](https://ref.ly/Esth1:17) · [EST 1:18](https://ref.ly/Esth1:18) · [EST 2:22](https://ref.ly/Esth2:22) · [EST 4:4](https://ref.ly/Esth4:4) · [EST 5:2](https://ref.ly/Esth5:2) · [EST 5:3](https://ref.ly/Esth5:3) · [EST 5:12](https://ref.ly/Esth5:12) · [EST 7:1](https://ref.ly/Esth7:1) · [EST 7:2](https://ref.ly/Esth7:2) · [EST 7:3](https://ref.ly/Esth7:3) · [EST 7:5](https://ref.ly/Esth7:5) · [EST 7:6](https://ref.ly/Esth7:6) · [EST 7:7](https://ref.ly/Esth7:7) · [EST 7:8](https://ref.ly/Esth7:8) · [EST 8:1](https://ref.ly/Esth8:1) · [EST 8:7](https://ref.ly/Esth8:7) · [EST 9:12](https://ref.ly/Esth9:12) · [EST 9:29](https://ref.ly/Esth9:29) · [EST 9:31](https://ref.ly/Esth9:31) · [SNG 6:8](https://ref.ly/Song6:8) · [SNG 6:9](https://ref.ly/Song6:9) · [DAN 5:10](https://ref.ly/Dan5:10) · [DAN 5:10](https://ref.ly/Dan5:10)

1 JER 7:18 The original reading of the passages in Jeremiah is the "queen of heaven", i.e. the mother godess. cf. versiones: LXX, Vg, Aq, Sym, Th. The cakes offered to the deity in Jer 7:18; 44:19 may have been star\-shaped. The most likely candidates for the title in Jer 7 and 44 are the West Semitic Astarte and the Mesopotamian Ishtar, both of whom are fertility goddesses identified with Venus, the morning and evening star. Both were offered cakes as part of their cult, though it is only Ishtar in whose image cakes were baked and whose cult held a particular appeal for women. Ishtar was known as the queen or lady of heaven. Queen of Heaven in Jeremiah is therefore a syncretistic deity, combining aspects of Astarte and Ishtar.

A. Salvesen

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 10:1; 1 Kings 10:4; 1 Kings 10:10; 1 Kings 10:13; 2 Chronicles 9:1; 2 Chronicles 9:3; 2 Chronicles 9:9; 2 Chronicles 9:12; Esther 1:9; Esther 1:11; Esther 1:12; Esther 1:15; Esther 1:16; Esther 1:17; Esther 1:18; Esther 2:22; Esther 4:4; Esther 5:2; Esther 5:3; Esther 5:12; Esther 7:1; Esther 7:2; Esther 7:3; Esther 7:5; Esther 7:6; Esther 7:7; Esther 7:8; Esther 8:1; Esther 8:7; Esther 9:12; Esther 9:29; Esther 9:31; Song of Songs 6:8; Song of Songs 6:9; Daniel 5:10

## מְלֶכֶת (id: 004069000000000)

מְלֶכֶת
=======

מְלֶכֶת (H4446) [SAHD](SADH@http://www.sahd.div.ed.ac.uk/_media/lexeme:pdf:ham-malka-salvesen_a-oxf-1999.pdf)

noun f · מלך

Names of Deities

**Collocation:** מְלֶכֶת הַשָּׁמַיִם

Queen of Heaven literally: Queen of Heaven; hence: \= epithet of a female deity, possibly to be identified with Ishtar or Ashtoreth; ► worshipped by the Israelites The "Queen of Heaven" in Jeremiah is the mother godess. cf. versiones: LXX, Vg, Aq, Sym, Th. A likely identification of the title with the West Semitic Ashtoreth or the Mesopotamian Ishtar is based on the cakes offered to the deity in Jer 7:18; 44:19 which may have been star\-shaped. Both Ashtoreth and Ishtar are fertility goddesses identified with Venus, the morning and evening star. Both were offered cakes as part of their cult, though it is only Ishtar in whose image cakes were baked and whose cult held a particular appeal for women. Ishtar was known as the queen or lady of heaven. Queen of Heaven in Jeremiah is therefore a syncretistic deity, combining aspects of Ashtoreth and Ishtar.

[JER 7:18](https://ref.ly/Jer7:18) · [JER 44:17](https://ref.ly/Jer44:17) · [JER 44:18](https://ref.ly/Jer44:18) · [JER 44:19](https://ref.ly/Jer44:19) · [JER 44:25](https://ref.ly/Jer44:25)

A. Salvesen

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 7:18; Jeremiah 44:17; Jeremiah 44:18; Jeremiah 44:19; Jeremiah 44:25

## מלל (id: 004071000000000)

מלל
===

מלל (H4448 · H4449 · H4135 · H5243a)

verb · מִלָּה · מְלִילָה

Dry \| verb nif, verb qal, verb pi \| ≈ יבשׁ

to wither, to fade \= process by which plants and their products become devoid of moisture, and therefore lose color and bend over ◄ as if in mourning, ► which may ultimately result in death if circumstances do not change; ≈ associated with drought and destruction, often as punishment from God

[JOB 14:2](https://ref.ly/Job14:2) · [JOB 18:16](https://ref.ly/Job18:16) · [PSA 90:6](https://ref.ly/Ps90:6)

Insignificant \| verb nif, verb qal, verb hit \| ≈ מאס · מכך · נבל

to wither, to fade literally: to wither; hence: \= process by which humans gradually lose their power and social status

[JOB 24:24](https://ref.ly/Job24:24)1 · [PSA 37:2](https://ref.ly/Ps37:2) · [PSA 58:8](https://ref.ly/Ps58:8)2

verb

Stance \| verb qal

to make signs, to signal \= action by which humans move parts of their bodies in order to pass on information without other people noticing

[PRO 6:13](https://ref.ly/Prov6:13)

Speak \| verb pi, verb pa

to speak, to say \= action by which humans, and personified objects utter words; usually not directly followed by the actual words that are spoken

[GEN 21:7](https://ref.ly/Gen21:7) · [JOB 8:2](https://ref.ly/Job8:2) · [JOB 33:3](https://ref.ly/Job33:3) · [PSA 106:2](https://ref.ly/Ps106:2) · [DAN 6:22](https://ref.ly/Dan6:22) · [DAN 7:8](https://ref.ly/Dan7:8) · [DAN 7:11](https://ref.ly/Dan7:11) · [DAN 7:20](https://ref.ly/Dan7:20) · [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25)

verb

Non\-Whole \| verb qal, verb nif

to circumcise, circumcision \= action by which someone removes the foreskin of the penis, \+ preferably with a flint knife, as a sign of the covenant between YHWH and Abraham's descendants; ≈ considered an outward change for the good that should be accompanied by an inward change in attitude as well

[GEN 17:11](https://ref.ly/Gen17:11) · [JOS 5:2](https://ref.ly/Josh5:2)

1 JOB 24:24 NJPS "wither" \| RSV NIV NRSV GNT ESV "be cut off" \= \[3a] \| REB "droop" 2 PSA 58:8 RSV NJB NRSV "wither" \| NIV ESV "be blunted" NJPS "be cut down" \= \[3a]

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Job 14:2; Job 18:16; Psalms 90:6; Job 24:24; Psalms 37:2; Psalms 58:8; Proverbs 6:13; Genesis 21:7; Job 8:2; Job 33:3; Psalms 106:2; Daniel 6:22; Daniel 7:8; Daniel 7:11; Daniel 7:20; Daniel 7:25; Genesis 17:11; Joshua 5:2

מנה
===

verb · מֵן · מָנָה · מֹנֶה · מְנִי · מְנָת

Measure \| verb qal, verb pe'al, verb nif \| ≈ מִסְפָּר · ספר

to count, to number \= action with which one determines or estimates the number of a collection of persons, objects, or events

[GEN 13:16](https://ref.ly/Gen13:16) · [GEN 13:16](https://ref.ly/Gen13:16) · [NUM 23:10](https://ref.ly/Num23:10) · [2SA 24:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:1) · [1KI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:8) · [1KI 8:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:5) · [1KI 20:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:25) · [2KI 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:11) · [1CH 21:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:1) · [1CH 21:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:17) · [1CH 27:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:24) · [2CH 5:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:6) · [PSA 90:12](https://ref.ly/Ps90:12) · [PSA 147:4](https://ref.ly/Ps147:4) · [ECC 1:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:15) · [JER 33:13](https://ref.ly/Jer33:13) · [DAN 5:26](https://ref.ly/Dan5:26)

Compare \| verb nif

to be counted among \= state in which humans hold the opinion that a particular person is to be identified with, considered similar to, or to be equated with another person or group

[ISA 53:12](https://ref.ly/Isa53:12)

Engage \| verb pi

to be apportioned, to be allotted \= process by which humans undergo a certain event, ◄ as if it were measured out and assigned to them

[JOB 7:3](https://ref.ly/Job7:3)

Engage \| verb qal

to assign \= causative action by which deities causes humans to undergo a certain event, ◄ as if by measuring it out and assigning it to them

[ISA 65:12](https://ref.ly/Isa65:12)

Quantity \| verb pi

to assign \= causative action by which people in authority make a decision regarding a certain quantity of food items to be given to individuals or groups of people on a regular basis

[DAN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Dan1:5) · [DAN 1:10](https://ref.ly/Dan1:10)

Role \| verb pi, verb pa, verb pu'al

to appoint \= causative action by which humans or deities give someone else a certain responsibility or position of authority with regards to an object, a group of people, or a certain task

[1CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:29) · [EZR 7:25](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:25) · [PSA 61:8](https://ref.ly/Ps61:8) · [DAN 1:11](https://ref.ly/Dan1:11) · [DAN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Dan2:24) · [DAN 2:49](https://ref.ly/Dan2:49) · [DAN 3:12](https://ref.ly/Dan3:12) · [JON 2:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:1) · [JON 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:6) · [JON 4:7](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:7) · [JON 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:8)

## מִנְחָה (id: 004122000000000)

מִנְחָה
=======

מִנְחָה (H4503 · A4504)

noun f

Objects

gift, tribute \= objects given to a person of high status; ► sometimes this is done on a voluntary basis as a show of respect for the recipient and in order to win that person's favor; in other cases this is done to fulfill the requirements imposed by the recipient

## מנע (id: 004133000000000)

מנע
===

מנע (H4513)

verb · יִמְנָע

Possess \| verb qal

to withhold, to refuse, to hold back, to keep \= action by which humans or deities are unwilling to give or sell something to (other) humans

[1KI 20:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:7) · [NEH 9:20](https://ref.ly/Neh9:20) · [JOB 22:7](https://ref.ly/Job22:7) · [JOB 31:16](https://ref.ly/Job31:16) · [PRO 11:26](https://ref.ly/Prov11:26)

Associate \| verb qal

to withhold, to deny literally: to withhold; hence: \= action by which humans refuse to allow other humans to marry their daughter

[2SA 13:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:13)

Birth \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מנע פְּרִי בֶּטֶן

to withhold the fruit of the womb, to deny (someone) motherhood literally: to withhold the fruit of the womb (from someone); hence: \= causative action by which deities keep women from becoming pregnant

[GEN 30:2](https://ref.ly/Gen30:2)

Non\-Engage \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מנע רֶגֶל מִנְּתִיבָה

to keep (one's) foot from (someone else's) path, to stay clear of (someone's) ways literally: to withhold (one's) foot from (someone else's) path; hence: \= action by which humans refuse to engage in a certain kind of behavior

[PRO 1:15](https://ref.ly/Prov1:15)

Non\-Engage \| verb qal

to keep, to restrain to withhold (something or someone from doing something); hence: \= action by which humans or deities keep (other) humans or objects from being engaged in a certain event

[NUM 22:16](https://ref.ly/Num22:16) · [1SA 25:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:26) · [1SA 25:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:34) · [JER 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jer2:25) · [JER 31:16](https://ref.ly/Jer31:16) · [JER 48:10](https://ref.ly/Jer48:10)

Non\-Flow \| verb qal

to restrain literally: to withhold; hence: \= action by which deities keep a river from flowing

[EZK 31:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:15)

Non\-Happen \| verb qal, verb nif \| ≈ היה · נטה \| ≠ נתן

to withhold, to deny literally: to withhold; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause a certain event not to take place

[NUM 24:11](https://ref.ly/Num24:11) · [JOB 38:15](https://ref.ly/Job38:15) · [PSA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Ps21:3) · [PSA 84:12](https://ref.ly/Ps84:12) · [PRO 3:27](https://ref.ly/Prov3:27) · [PRO 23:13](https://ref.ly/Prov23:13) · [PRO 30:7](https://ref.ly/Prov30:7) · [JER 3:3](https://ref.ly/Jer3:3) · [JER 5:25](https://ref.ly/Jer5:25) · [JOL 1:13](https://ref.ly/Joel1:13) · [AMO 4:7](https://ref.ly/Amos4:7)

Rejoice \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מנע לֵב מִשִּׂמְחָה

to keep (one's) heart from enjoyment literally: to withhold (one's) heart from joy; hence: \= action by which humans refuse to engage in activities that bring joy

[ECC 2:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:10)

Space \| verb qal

to hold (something somewhere) literally: to withhold (something somewhere); hence: \= action by which humans keep an object in a certain place and are unwilling to remove it

[JOB 20:13](https://ref.ly/Job20:13)

Non\-Speak \| verb qal

to hold back literally: to withhold; hence: \= action by which humans are unwilling to pass on a message

[JER 42:4](https://ref.ly/Jer42:4)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 20:7; Nehemiah 9:20; Job 22:7; Job 31:16; Proverbs 11:26; 2 Samuel 13:13; Genesis 30:2; Proverbs 1:15; Numbers 22:16; 1 Samuel 25:26; 1 Samuel 25:34; Jeremiah 2:25; Jeremiah 31:16; Jeremiah 48:10; Ezekiel 31:15; Numbers 24:11; Job 38:15; Psalms 21:3; Psalms 84:12; Proverbs 3:27; Proverbs 23:13; Proverbs 30:7; Jeremiah 3:3; Jeremiah 5:25; Joel 1:13; Amos 4:7; Ecclesiastes 2:10; Job 20:13; Jeremiah 42:4

## מְנָת (id: 004141000000000)

מְנָת
=====

מְנָת (H4521)

noun f · מנה

Objects

part, portion \= one part of an object (or range of objects) that has been divided into several portions

[2CH 31:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:3) · [2CH 31:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:4) · [NEH 12:44](https://ref.ly/Neh12:44) · [NEH 12:47](https://ref.ly/Neh12:47) · [NEH 13:10](https://ref.ly/Neh13:10) · [PSA 16:5](https://ref.ly/Ps16:5)

Possess

portion, prey literally: portion; hence: \= something available for a wild animal to eat, ◄ as if it were its alotted share of a larger quantity

[PSA 63:11](https://ref.ly/Ps63:11)

Engage

portion, lot literally: portion; hence: \= an event (or a range of events) that deities cause humans to undergo, ◄ as if by measuring it out and assigning it to them

[PSA 11:6](https://ref.ly/Ps11:6) · [JER 13:25](https://ref.ly/Jer13:25)

[1CH 22:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:3) · [2CH 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:9) · [ISA 41:7](https://ref.ly/Isa41:7)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 22:3; 2 Chronicles 3:9; Isaiah 41:7


## מסס (id: 004169000000000)

מסס
===

מסס (H4549)

verb · מָס · מַסָּה · תֶּמֶס · מאס

Soft \| verb nif

to melt, to dissolve \= process by which substances become soft, ◄ often as a result of a higher temperature

[EXO 16:21](https://ref.ly/Exod16:21) · [JDG 15:14](https://ref.ly/Judg15:14) · [PSA 68:3](https://ref.ly/Ps68:3) · [PSA 97:5](https://ref.ly/Ps97:5) · [EZK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:12) · [MIC 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mic1:4) · [NAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Nah2:11)

Afraid \| verb nif

**Collocation:** לֵב מסס · לֵבָב מסס

to lose heart literally: (one's) heart melts; hence: \= state in which humans lose all courage

[JOS 2:11](https://ref.ly/Josh2:11) · [JOS 5:1](https://ref.ly/Josh5:1) · [JOS 7:5](https://ref.ly/Josh7:5) · [2SA 17:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:10) · [2SA 17:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:10) · [PSA 22:15](https://ref.ly/Ps22:15) · [ISA 13:7](https://ref.ly/Isa13:7) · [ISA 19:1](https://ref.ly/Isa19:1)

Afraid \| verb hif, verb nif

**Collocation:** מסס לֵבָב

to make (someone's) heart melt literally: to make (someone's) heart melt; hence: \= causative process by which humans cause other humans to lose all courage

[DEU 1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:28) · [DEU 20:8](https://ref.ly/Deut20:8)

Flow \| verb nif

to flow literally: to melt; hence: \= process by which liquids flow from an object as if the object itself is melting away

[ISA 34:3](https://ref.ly/Isa34:3)

Non\-Exist \| verb qal

to waste away literally: to melt; hence: \= process by which people or objects to vanish from existence

[PSA 112:10](https://ref.ly/Ps112:10) · [ISA 10:18](https://ref.ly/Isa10:18)

1 1SA 15:9 LXX ἐξουδενωμένον RSV NJPS NJB GNT ESV "worthless" REB "of no value" \| MT NIV "weak"

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 16:21; Judges 15:14; Psalms 68:3; Psalms 97:5; Ezekiel 21:12; Micah 1:4; Nahum 2:11; Joshua 2:11; Joshua 5:1; Joshua 7:5; 2 Samuel 17:10; Psalms 22:15; Isaiah 13:7; Isaiah 19:1; Deuteronomy 1:28; Deuteronomy 20:8; Isaiah 34:3; Psalms 112:10; Isaiah 10:18

מַסַּע
======

מַסַּע (H4550)

noun m · נסע

Move

march, journey \= action by which a relatively large company of humans, together with their possessions, moves from one temporary residence to another

Move

breaking camp, setting out \= causative action by which a leader causes a relatively large company of humans, together with their possessions, to depart from a place where they had set up a temporary residence

## מַסָּע (id: 004171000000000)

noun m · נסע

quarry a large pit from which stones are cut

noun m

Weapons

dart exact meaning uncertain; probably \= a small hand\-held weapon, sharpened to a point on one end, and thrown

## מִסְפֵּד (id: 004173000000000)

מִסְפֵּד
========

מִסְפֵּד (H4553)

noun m · ספד

Grieve \| ≈ אֵבֶל · בְּכִי \| ≠ מָחֹול

mourning \= mourning ritual for someone who has died, or for another tragic event; this may include wailing, tearing one's clothes, putting on sackcloth, covering oneself with dust or ashes, fasting or eating specific food, cutting oneself, shaving one's head, covering part of one's face, etc.; this usually takes place during a specific period of time and ends after the burial has taken place, but it may also be repeated in later days to commemorate a tragic event

[GEN 50:10](https://ref.ly/Gen50:10) · [EST 4:3](https://ref.ly/Esth4:3) · [PSA 30:12](https://ref.ly/Ps30:12) · [ISA 22:12](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12) · [JER 6:26](https://ref.ly/Jer6:26) · [JER 48:38](https://ref.ly/Jer48:38) · [EZK 27:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:31) · [JOL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Joel2:12) · [AMO 5:16](https://ref.ly/Amos5:16) · [AMO 5:16](https://ref.ly/Amos5:16) · [AMO 5:17](https://ref.ly/Amos5:17) · [MIC 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mic1:8) · [MIC 1:11](https://ref.ly/Mic1:11) · [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10) · [ZEC 12:11](https://ref.ly/Zech12:11) · [ZEC 12:11](https://ref.ly/Zech12:11)

מִסְפָּר
========

noun m · ספר

Know

[JDG 7:15](https://ref.ly/Judg7:15) · [1CH 11:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:11) · [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1) · [JOB 31:37](https://ref.ly/Job31:37)

Measure

counting \= action by which humans determine the number of objects

[1CH 9:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:28) · [1CH 9:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:28) · [EZR 8:34](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:34)

Frequency

**Collocation:** אֵין מִסְפָּר

counting literally: there is no counting (of it); hence: \= state in which events cannot be counted or measured because of their frequency or intensity

[JOB 5:9](https://ref.ly/Job5:9) · [JOB 9:10](https://ref.ly/Job9:10) · [PSA 40:13](https://ref.ly/Ps40:13) · [PSA 147:5](https://ref.ly/Ps147:5) · [JER 2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:32)

Quantity

**Collocation:** אֵין מִסְפָּר

counting literally: there is no counting (of it); hence: \= state in which objects cannot be counted or measured because of their abundance

verb · מְעַט

Quantity \| verb qal

to become few, to decrease \= state in which humans or objects are relatively few in number

[EXO 12:4](https://ref.ly/Exod12:4) · [PSA 107:39](https://ref.ly/Ps107:39) · [PRO 13:11](https://ref.ly/Prov13:11) · [ECC 12:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:3) · [ISA 21:17](https://ref.ly/Isa21:17) · [JER 29:6](https://ref.ly/Jer29:6) · [JER 30:19](https://ref.ly/Jer30:19)

Quantity \| verb hif

to make few \= causative action by which deities cause humans or objects to be relatively few in number

[LEV 26:22](https://ref.ly/Lev26:22) · [PSA 107:38](https://ref.ly/Ps107:38) · [JER 10:24](https://ref.ly/Jer10:24) · [EZK 29:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:15)

Frequency \| verb qal

to be few \= state in which events are few in frequency

[LEV 25:16](https://ref.ly/Lev25:16)

Lax \| verb qal

to be little \= state in which an event happens with little intensity

[NEH 9:32](https://ref.ly/Neh9:32)

Lax \| verb hif

to do little \= action by which humans perform an event with little intensity

[EXO 16:17](https://ref.ly/Exod16:17) · [EXO 16:18](https://ref.ly/Exod16:18) · [EXO 30:15](https://ref.ly/Exod30:15) · [NUM 11:32](https://ref.ly/Num11:32) · [NUM 26:54](https://ref.ly/Num26:54) · [NUM 33:54](https://ref.ly/Num33:54) · [NUM 35:8](https://ref.ly/Num35:8) · [2KI 4:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:3)

Lax \| verb hif

to reduce, to lower \= causative action by which humans cause events to have little intensity

[LEV 25:16](https://ref.ly/Lev25:16)

Reinier de Blois

## מְעִיל (id: 004214000000000)

מְעִיל
======

מְעִיל (H4598)

noun m

Clothing \| ≈ בֶּגֶד

robe, mantle, cloak \= long garment, hanging down to the ankles, ◄ usually made of wool, ► and worn over the tunic; it is not clear whether it had sleeves or not, nor whether it was open or closed in the front; ≈ certain attitudes and emotions are often described in terms of such a garment, e.g. "he wore fury like a robe"

spring \= an opening in the earth that gives access to the subterranean water which either comes to the surface or can be drawn from it; ≈ its water is regarded as a source of blessing

Flow \| ≈ פֶּלֶג

spring (symbolizing semen) \= process by which water flows from a spring; ≈ only used figuratively to refer to human semen flowing from the body

## מעל (id: 004220000000000)

מעל
===

מעל (H4603)

verb · מַעַל

Unfaithful \| verb qal \| ≈ חטא

to commit a violation, to be unfaithful \= action by which humans do something to (other) humans or deities that is not in line with the behavior that they committed themselves to

מַעֲלָה
=======

מַעֲלָה (H4609)

noun f · עלה

Move

to go up, to climb up, to mount \= action by which humans, animals or objects go to a place that is situated at a higher elevation, by foot, carried by hand, or with the help of a vehicle

[EZR 7:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:9)

Great

ascending, rising (in status) meaning unsure; literally: coming up; hence: \= process by which humans or deities increase in status and importance

Buildings

step (of staircase) \= one of a set of fixed steps leading up to a higher area

Plants \| ≈ תֶּבֶן · חֲשַׁשׁ

chaff \= debris and husks crushed and separated from grain and carried away by the wind when threshing and winnowing ≈ associated especially with the transient nature and disappearance of evil people and enemies.

[JOB 21:18](https://ref.ly/Job21:18) · [PSA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Ps1:4) · [PSA 35:5](https://ref.ly/Ps35:5) · [ISA 17:13](https://ref.ly/Isa17:13) · [ISA 29:5](https://ref.ly/Isa29:5) · [ISA 41:15](https://ref.ly/Isa41:15) · [HOS 13:3](https://ref.ly/Hos13:3) · [ZEP 2:2](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:2)

Anne Garber Kompaore

* **Associated Passages:** Job 21:18; Psalms 1:4; Psalms 35:5; Isaiah 17:13; Isaiah 29:5; Isaiah 41:15; Hosea 13:3; Zephaniah 2:2


## מצא (id: 004288000000000)

מצא
===

מצא (H4672)

verb

Find \| verb qal, verb nif

to find \= process by which humans or animals discover the person, animal, object or location they were looking for

Able \| verb qal

**Collocation:** יָדַיִם מצא

to be able (to do something) literally: (one's) hands find (something to do); hence: \= state in which humans have the opportunity and ability to do something

[JDG 9:33](https://ref.ly/Judg9:33) · [1SA 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:7) · [ECC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:10)

Able \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מצא יָדַיִם

to be able to help literally: to find (one's) hands; \= state in which humans have the strength to come in action

[PSA 76:6](https://ref.ly/Ps76:6)

Associate \| verb qal, verb nif

to find (God) \= process by which humans on a spiritual journey find God

[DEU 4:29](https://ref.ly/Deut4:29) · [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9) · [2CH 15:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:2) · [2CH 15:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:4) · [2CH 15:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:15) · [JOB 37:23](https://ref.ly/Job37:23) · [PSA 32:6](https://ref.ly/Ps32:6) · [ISA 55:6](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6) · [ISA 65:1](https://ref.ly/Isa65:1) · [JER 29:13](https://ref.ly/Jer29:13) · [JER 29:14](https://ref.ly/Jer29:14) · [HOS 5:6](https://ref.ly/Hos5:6)

Chastise \| verb qal

to find (someone) out literally: (one's) sin will find (someone); hence: \= process by which humans eventually suffer the penalty for the wrong they have done

[NUM 32:23](https://ref.ly/Num32:23)

Confident \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מצא לֵב

to find courage literally: to find heart; hence: \= process by which humans are able to overcome their fear

[2SA 7:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:27) · [1CH 17:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:25)1

Control \| verb hif

**Collocation:** מצא בְּיַָד

to give in (someone's) hand literally: to cause (someone) to find themselves in the hand (of someone else); hence: \= causative process by which humans cuase other humans to be in the power of a thrid party

[2SA 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:8) · [ZEC 11:6](https://ref.ly/Zech11:6)

Dead \| verb qal

**Collocation:** מצא קֶבֶר

to die literally: to find the grave; hence: \= process by which humans eventually die

[JOB 3:22](https://ref.ly/Job3:22)

Engage \| verb qal

to find, to get, to acquire literally: to find (a certain condition); hence: \= process by which humans eventually reach a certain state or condition

Happen \| verb qal

to come upon literally: (an event) finds (people); \= process by which humans eventually undergo a certain event, ◄ as if that event had been searching for them

Happen \| verb nif

to happen, to take place literally: (a condition) is found (in someone); hence: \= process by which humans are eventually discovered to have a certain condition or to be involved in a certain activity

[1SA 25:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:28) · [1KI 1:52](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:52) · [1KI 14:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:13) · [PRO 10:13](https://ref.ly/Prov10:13) · [JER 11:9](https://ref.ly/Jer11:9) · [MIC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Mic1:13) · [MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6)

Happen \| verb hif

to cause to happen literally: to cause (someone) to find (an event); hence: \= causative process by which deities case humans to eventually undergo a certain event, ◄ as if that event had been sent out to search for them

[JOB 34:11](https://ref.ly/Job34:11) · [JOB 37:13](https://ref.ly/Job37:13)

Hold \| verb hif

to hand, to pass literally: to cause (someone) to find (something); hence: \= action by which humans hand over objects to other humans

[LEV 9:12](https://ref.ly/Lev9:12) · [LEV 9:13](https://ref.ly/Lev9:13) · [LEV 9:18](https://ref.ly/Lev9:18)

Know \| verb qal, verb nif

to learn, to find out literally: to find (in someone); hence: \= process by which humans come upon certain information

Possess \| verb qal

**Collocation:** יָד מצא

מַצָּב

Space

[JOS 4:3](https://ref.ly/Josh4:3) · [JOS 4:9](https://ref.ly/Josh4:9)

Groups \| Soldiers

garrison literally: placed (group of people); hence: \= a body of troops

Role \| ≈ מַעֲמָד

office, position literally: placed; hence: \= state in which humans have been assigned a certain role
