## מָצֹוד (id: 004298000000000)

מָצֹוד
======

מָצֹוד (H4679 · H4685a · H4685b · H4685c · H4685d · H4686a · H4686b) — מְצָד, מְצוּדָה, מְצֹודָה

noun c · צוד

Control

**Collocation:** מְצוּדָה

prey, victim \= animal hunted by humans or other animals ► for food; ≈ often extended metaphorically to people

[EZK 13:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:21)

Traps \| ≈ אֵסוּר · חֵרֶם · רֶשֶׁת

**Collocation:** מָצֹוד · מְצֹודָה · מְצוּדָה

(trammel) net \= combination of two or three nets in layers, usually a small\-mesh inner net sandwiched between two nets of looser mesh; ► set out vertically in the water, held in place by floats on the surface of the water and weighted down by a single weighted rope at the bottom; left in place for several hours to give time for fish to accumulate in it; ≈ associated with aggression and control

[JOB 19:6](https://ref.ly/Job19:6)1 · [PSA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Ps66:11) · [ECC 7:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:26) · [ECC 9:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:12) · [EZK 12:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:13) · [EZK 17:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:20)

noun f

Fortifications \| ≈ מִלֹּוא · מִשְׂגָּב

**Collocation:** מְצָד · מָצֹוד · מְצוּדָה · מְצֹודָה

stronghold, fortress \= area shaped and located in such a way that it is hard to access and can be easily defended against enemies; ◄ this can be a natural stronghold in the mountains (e.g. caves) or a construction erected by humans in populated areas; ≈ often associated with security and sometimes used as an epithet for leaders or deities that are considered providers of security

[JDG 6:2](https://ref.ly/Judg6:2) · [1SA 22:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:4) · [1SA 22:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:5) · [1SA 23:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:14) · [1SA 23:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:19) · [1SA 24:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:1) · [1SA 24:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:23) · [2SA 5:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:7) · [2SA 5:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:9) · [2SA 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:17) · [2SA 23:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:14) · [1CH 11:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:5) · [1CH 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:7) · [1CH 11:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:16) · [1CH 12:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:9) · [1CH 12:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:17) · [JOB 39:28](https://ref.ly/Job39:28) · [PRO 12:12](https://ref.ly/Prov12:12)2 · [ISA 29:7](https://ref.ly/Isa29:7) · [ISA 33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:16) · [JER 48:41](https://ref.ly/Jer48:41) · [JER 51:30](https://ref.ly/Jer51:30) · [EZK 19:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:9) · [EZK 33:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:27)

Titles

Stronghold, Fortress literally: (God as one's) stronghold, fortress; hence: \= a title of God as the one providing security for those who follow him

[2SA 22:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:2) · [PSA 18:3](https://ref.ly/Ps18:3) · [PSA 31:3](https://ref.ly/Ps31:3) · [PSA 31:4](https://ref.ly/Ps31:4) · [PSA 71:3](https://ref.ly/Ps71:3) · [PSA 91:2](https://ref.ly/Ps91:2) · [PSA 144:2](https://ref.ly/Ps144:2)

noun m

Weapons

siege\-works \= construction of which the exact form is unknown, ► built in order to gain access to a city during a siege

[JOB 19:6](https://ref.ly/Job19:6)1 · [ECC 9:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:14)3

===

מצץ (H4711)

verb · מַצָּה

Ingest \| verb qal \| ≈ ינק

to suck \= action by which newborn children drink milk from their mother's breasts

[ISA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Isa66:11)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 66:11


## מֵצַר (id: 004319000000000)

מֵצַר
=====

מֵצַר (H4712)

noun m · צרר

Afraid

distress \= a state in which humans experience extreme anxiety, ◄ as a result of difficult circumstances, as if one were confined to a narrow and cramped space from which there is no escape

[PSA 116:3](https://ref.ly/Ps116:3) · [PSA 118:5](https://ref.ly/Ps118:5) · [LAM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Lam1:3)

Graham S. Ogden

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 116:3; Psalms 118:5; Lamentations 1:3


## מִצְרִי (id: 004320000000000)

מִצְרִי
=======

מִצְרִי (H4713)

adj · מִצְרַיִם

Names of Groups

Egyptian \= descendant or inhabitant of Mizraim (Egypt)

## מִצְרַיִם (id: 004321000000000)

מִצְרַיִם
=========

מִצְרַיִם (H4714)

name · מִצְרִי · אָבֵל מִצְרַיִם

Names of People

Mizraim, Egypt \= man; ◄ son of Ham; ► regarded as founder of the Egyptian nation

[GEN 10:6](https://ref.ly/Gen10:6) · [GEN 10:13](https://ref.ly/Gen10:13) · [1CH 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:8) · [1CH 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:11)

Names of Groups \| Names of Locations \| ≈ מָצֹור

## מַקֶּבֶת (id: 004324000000000)

מַקֶּבֶת
========

מַקֶּבֶת (H4717 · H4718a · H4718b)

noun f · נקב

Tools \| ≈ גַּרְזֶן

hammer \= instrument of about 30 centimeters long, consisting of a handle, usually made of wood, to which was attached a stone, wooden, or (less frequently) metal head; ► used for many purposes, including the breaking or dressing of building stones, pounding nails or pegs into wood, or pounding pegs into the ground; also used by blacksmiths to shape hot metal

[JDG 4:21](https://ref.ly/Judg4:21) · [1KI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:7) · [ISA 44:12](https://ref.ly/Isa44:12) · [JER 10:4](https://ref.ly/Jer10:4)

\| ≈ צוּר

**Collocation:** מַקֶּבֶת בֹּור

quarry \= large pit from which stones are cut

[ISA 51:1](https://ref.ly/Isa51:1)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 4:21; 1 Kings 6:7; Isaiah 44:12; Jeremiah 10:4; Isaiah 51:1

## מָקֹור (id: 004332000000000)

מָקֹור
======

מָקֹור (H4726)

noun m · קור

Waterbodies \| ≈ יָם · מַעְיָן

spring \= an opening in the earth that gives access to the subterranean water which either comes to the surface or can be drawn from it; ≈ its water is regarded as a source of life and well\-being

[PRO 25:26](https://ref.ly/Prov25:26) · [JER 51:36](https://ref.ly/Jer51:36) · [HOS 13:15](https://ref.ly/Hos13:15) · [ZEC 13:1](https://ref.ly/Zech13:1)

Alive \| ≈ אֹור

**Collocation:** מְקֹור חַיִּים

spring of life, source of life literally: spring of life; hence: \= a reference to God or wisdom as a sources of life, ◄ as a spring is a source of life to a community

[PSA 36:10](https://ref.ly/Ps36:10) · [PRO 10:11](https://ref.ly/Prov10:11) · [PRO 13:14](https://ref.ly/Prov13:14) · [PRO 14:27](https://ref.ly/Prov14:27) · [PRO 16:22](https://ref.ly/Prov16:22)

Alive

**Collocation:** מְקֹור מַיִם חַיִּים

spring of life, source of life literally: spring of living water; hence: \= a reference to God as a source of life, ◄ as a spring is a source of life to a community

[JER 2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:13) · [JER 17:13](https://ref.ly/Jer17:13)

Flow

flow (of blood) literally: spring (of blood); hence: \= process by which blood flows from a woman's body during her menstruation

[LEV 12:7](https://ref.ly/Lev12:7) · [LEV 20:18](https://ref.ly/Lev20:18) · [LEV 20:18](https://ref.ly/Lev20:18)

People

source, fountain literally: spring; hence: \= a reference to a person or group to whom a nation retraces its origins

[PSA 68:27](https://ref.ly/Ps68:27)1

People

source literally: spring; hence: \= part of the body from which liquids flow, such as tears

[JER 8:23](https://ref.ly/Jer8:23)

People

spring, fountain literally: (one's) spring; hence: \= an euphemistic reference to a man's genitals

[PRO 5:18](https://ref.ly/Prov5:18)

Wise

**Collocation:** מְקֹור חָכְמָה

spring of wisdom, source of wisdom literally: spring of wisdom; hence: \= a place from where wisdom is acquired

[PRO 18:4](https://ref.ly/Prov18:4)

1 PSA 68:27 RSV NJPS NRSV ESV \| NIV REB "assembly" \| NJB "foundation"

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 25:26; Jeremiah 51:36; Hosea 13:15; Zechariah 13:1; Psalms 36:10; Proverbs 10:11; Proverbs 13:14; Proverbs 14:27; Proverbs 16:22; Jeremiah 2:13; Jeremiah 17:13; Leviticus 12:7; Leviticus 20:18; Psalms 68:27; Jeremiah 8:23; Proverbs 5:18; Proverbs 18:4

## מַקֵּל (id: 004339000000000)

מַקֵּל
======

מַקֵּל (H4731)

noun m

Trees

branch either the branch of a living tree or a branch that has been cut off in order to be used as a staff or rod

[GEN 30:37](https://ref.ly/Gen30:37) · [GEN 30:37](https://ref.ly/Gen30:37) · [GEN 30:38](https://ref.ly/Gen30:38) · [GEN 30:39](https://ref.ly/Gen30:39) · [GEN 30:41](https://ref.ly/Gen30:41) · [GEN 30:41](https://ref.ly/Gen30:41) · [JER 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jer1:11)

Tools

stick, rod, club, scepter \= stick ◄ usually made of wood; ► attribute of a shepherd or a ruler, but also used for other purposes, such as beating, walking, divination, etc.; ≈ associated with shepherding, authority, punishment

[GEN 32:11](https://ref.ly/Gen32:11) · [EXO 12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:11) · [NUM 22:27](https://ref.ly/Num22:27) · [1SA 17:40](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:40) · [1SA 17:43](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:43) · [JER 48:17](https://ref.ly/Jer48:17) · [HOS 4:12](https://ref.ly/Hos4:12) · [ZEC 11:7](https://ref.ly/Zech11:7) · [ZEC 11:10](https://ref.ly/Zech11:10) · [ZEC 11:14](https://ref.ly/Zech11:14)

Weapons \| ≈ חֵץ · מָגֵן · צִנָּה · קֶשֶׁת · רֹמַח

**Collocation:** מַקֵּל יָד

war club, mace exact meaning unknown; possibly: \= a short wooden rod used for striking the enemy in hand\-to\-hand combat; it could be held with one hand and was used to strike the enemy on all parts of his body, particularly where he was unprotected by armor; possibly fitted with a heavy head made of stone or metal

[EZK 39:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:9)1

1 EZK 39:9 NIV NJPS GNT CEV ESV "(war) club" \| NJB "javelin" \| REB "throwing\-stick" \| RSV NRSV "handpike"

Reinier de Blois

divination \= action by which humans communicate with the spirit world using a variety of means such as inspecting the liver of animals, shaking arrows, consulting teraphim, or casting lots; ≈ usually regarded as an evil practice that is to be condemned; responsibility of a prophet or king

[EZK 12:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:24) · [EZK 13:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:7)

Graham S. Ogden

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 12:24; Ezekiel 13:7

מְרִיב בַּעַל (H4807 · H4810) — מְרִי־בַעַל, מְרִיב־בַּעַל

name

Names of People \| ≈ מְפִיבֹשֶׁת

Merib\-Baal \= man, also known as Mephibosheth; ◄ son of Jonathan and grandson of king Saul; tribe of Benjamin; ► father of Micah; allowed to live in David's palace out of loyalty towards Jonathan

[1CH 8:34](https://ref.ly/1Chr8:34) · [1CH 8:34](https://ref.ly/1Chr8:34) · [1CH 9:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:40) · [1CH 9:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:40)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 8:34; 1 Chronicles 9:40

verb · מֶמֶר · מַמְּרֹורִים · מַר · מֹר · מֹרָה · מְרִירוּת · מְרִירִי · מָרֹר · מְרֵרָה · מְרֹרָה · תַּמְרוּרִים

Bad \| verb qal

Angry \| Grieve \| verb pi

to attack bitterly, to assail bitterly literally: to act with bitterness; hence: \= action by which humans express their emotions of anger, resentment, and grief by engaging other humans in battle

Angry \| Grieve \| verb pi

**Collocation:** מרר בַּבֶּכִי

[ISA 22:4](https://ref.ly/Isa22:4)

Angry \| Grieve \| verb hif

to weep bitterly literally: to act with bitterness; hence: \= action by which humans express their emotions of anger, resentment, and grief by intense weeping

[ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10) · [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10)

Angry \| Grieve \| verb hif, verb pi

to make hard literally: to make bitter; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities cause (other) humans to experience emotions of anger, resentment, and grief by their actions

[EXO 1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:14) · [RUT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:20) · [JOB 27:2](https://ref.ly/Job27:2)

## מַשְׂאֵת (id: 004458000000000)

מַשְׂאֵת
========

מַשְׂאֵת (H4864)

noun f · נשׂא

Bear

bearing, carrying, suffering literally: lifting up; hence: \= process by which humans are held responsible for a certain action or for the well\-being of others

[ZEP 3:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:18)

Non\-Whole

to uproot, to pull out literally: lifting up; hence: \= causative action by which humans pull up plants or roots from the ground

[EZK 17:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:9)

Non\-Whole

portion, share literally: lifting up; hence: \= one part of an object (or range of objects) that has been divided into several portions

[GEN 43:34](https://ref.ly/Gen43:34) · [GEN 43:34](https://ref.ly/Gen43:34) · [GEN 43:34](https://ref.ly/Gen43:34)

Know

signal (of smoke) literally: lifting up; hence: \= an amount of rising smoke ► used as a means of communication

[JDG 20:38](https://ref.ly/Judg20:38) · [JDG 20:40](https://ref.ly/Judg20:40) · [JER 6:1](https://ref.ly/Jer6:1)

Non\-Possess

present, gift literally: lifting up; hence: \= causative action by which humans bring objects to (other) humans or deities as a gift

[2SA 11:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:8) · [EST 2:18](https://ref.ly/Esth2:18) · [JER 40:5](https://ref.ly/Jer40:5) · [EZK 20:40](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:40)

Non\-Possess

tax literally: lifting up; hence: \= an amount of money that is to be paid to a ruler in return for certain rights or privileges

[2CH 24:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:6) · [2CH 24:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:9) · [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11)

Pray \| ≈ תְּפִלָּה

to lift up (one's) hands, to lift up (one's) palms literally: lifting up; hence: \= action by which humans lift up their hands

[PSA 141:2](https://ref.ly/Ps141:2)

Reinier de Blois

## מְשֹׁואָה (id: 004468000000000)

מְשֹׁואָה
=========

מְשֹׁואָה (H4875)

noun f · שׁאה

Impact

**Collocation:** שֹׁואָה וּמְשֹׁואָה

total desolation, total destruction \= state in which buildings and infrastructure have been totally destroyed

[JOB 30:3](https://ref.ly/Job30:3) · [JOB 38:27](https://ref.ly/Job38:27) · [ZEP 1:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:15)

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Job 30:3; Job 38:27; Zephaniah 1:15

משׁח
====

verb · מִשְׁחָה · מָשְׁחָה · מָשִׁיחַ · מִמְשַׁח

Rub \| verb qal, verb nif

to spread, to apply, to rub, to oil, to paint, to anoint \= causative action by which humans apply a liquid like oil or paint to an object by pouring or rubbing ► for the purpose of cleaning, preserving, beautifying, eating, or for consecrating for religious service

to anoint \= causative action by which someone in authority confers the office of priest, king, or prophet on another person, by pouring oil on that person's head

מַשְׁחִית
=========

noun m · שׁחת

Impact

destruction \= causative action or process by which humans, deities, or animals cause (other) humans, animals, or objects to be affected by violence, destruction, death, putrefaction, pollution, blemishes, and other factors that may have a destructive or deadly impact

[EXO 12:13](https://ref.ly/Exod12:13) · [2CH 20:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:23) · [2CH 22:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:4) · [EZK 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:16) · [EZK 9:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:6) · [EZK 21:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:36) · [EZK 25:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:15) · [DAN 10:8](https://ref.ly/Dan10:8)

trap literally: destroyer; hence: \= contraption ► used for catching animals; ≈ associated with aggression and control

[JER 5:26](https://ref.ly/Jer5:26)

People

destroyer, raider, ravager \= a person engaged in actions that cause humans, animals, or objects to be affected by violence, destruction, death, putrefaction, pollution, blemishes, and other factors that may have a destructive or deadly impact

[EXO 12:23](https://ref.ly/Exod12:23) · [1SA 13:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:17) · [1SA 14:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:15) · [ISA 54:16](https://ref.ly/Isa54:16) · [JER 22:7](https://ref.ly/Jer22:7) · [JER 51:25](https://ref.ly/Jer51:25)

People

**Collocation:** בַּעַל מַשְׁחִית

destroyer literally: owner of destruction; hence: \= a person engaged in actions that cause humans, animals, or objects to be affected by violence, destruction, death, putrefaction, pollution, blemishes, and other factors that may have a destructive or deadly impact

[PRO 18:9](https://ref.ly/Prov18:9)

name

Names of Constructions

Mount of Destruction \= high place; ◄ located east of Jerusalem; ► destroyed by King Josiah

[2KI 23:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:13)

## מָשִׁיחַ (id: 004493000000000)

מָשִׁיחַ
========

מָשִׁיחַ (H4899) [SAHD](SAHD@http://www.sahd.div.ed.ac.uk/_media/lexeme:pdf:htg-mashiach-salvesen_a-oxf-1999.pdf)

noun m · משׁח

Leaders

anointed (one) \= a (non\-eschatological) ruler appointed by God and anointed with oil by a prophet or priest In the post\-exilic period an ideology of “bicephalic leadership” seems to have existed, argued on the basis of Zec 6\.12–14; 4\.2–3,11–14; 8\.9–17, Hag 1\.12–14\.This king\-priest duality is found strongly in Chronicles too, which evaluates Israelite history in these very terms of shared leadership. However, neither Jubilees nor Qumran cite Haggai or Zechariah in this context. For further reading cf. SAHD article.

[1SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:10) · [1SA 2:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:35) · [1SA 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:3) · [1SA 12:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:5) · [1SA 16:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:6) · [1SA 24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:7) · [1SA 24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:7) · [1SA 24:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:11) · [1SA 26:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:9) · [1SA 26:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:11) · [1SA 26:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:16) · [1SA 26:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:23) · [2SA 1:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:14) · [2SA 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:16) · [2SA 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:21) · [2SA 19:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:22) · [2SA 22:51](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:51) · [2SA 23:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:1) · [2CH 6:42](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:42) · [PSA 2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2) · [PSA 18:51](https://ref.ly/Ps18:51) · [PSA 20:7](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7) · [PSA 28:8](https://ref.ly/Ps28:8) · [PSA 84:10](https://ref.ly/Ps84:10) · [PSA 89:39](https://ref.ly/Ps89:39) · [PSA 89:52](https://ref.ly/Ps89:52) · [PSA 132:10](https://ref.ly/Ps132:10) · [PSA 132:17](https://ref.ly/Ps132:17) · [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1) · [LAM 4:20](https://ref.ly/Lam4:20) · [DAN 9:25](https://ref.ly/Dan9:25) · [DAN 9:26](https://ref.ly/Dan9:26) · [HAB 3:13](https://ref.ly/Hab3:13)

Priests

anointed (one) \= a (non\-eschatological) religious leader appointed by God and anointed with oil by a prophet or priest

[LEV 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:3) · [LEV 4:5](https://ref.ly/Lev4:5) · [LEV 4:16](https://ref.ly/Lev4:16) · [LEV 6:15](https://ref.ly/Lev6:15) · [1CH 16:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:22) · [PSA 105:15](https://ref.ly/Ps105:15)

A. Salvesen

## משׁך (id: 004494000000000)

משׁך
====

משׁך (H4900)

verb · מֶשֶׁךְ · מֹשְׁכֹות · מֶשֶׁךְ

Large \| verb pu'al

to be tall \= state in which humans are of more than average height1

[ISA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Isa18:2) · [ISA 18:7](https://ref.ly/Isa18:7)

Move \| verb qal

to go, to march \= action by which humans move as a group from one location to another

[EXO 12:21](https://ref.ly/Exod12:21) · [JDG 4:6](https://ref.ly/Judg4:6) · [JDG 5:14](https://ref.ly/Judg5:14) · [JDG 20:37](https://ref.ly/Judg20:37) · [JOB 21:33](https://ref.ly/Job21:33)

Move \| verb qal

to pull, to drag, to draw out \= causative action by which humans or animals move an object from one location to another, usually with some effort or difficulty

[GEN 37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:28) · [DEU 21:3](https://ref.ly/Deut21:3) · [JDG 4:7](https://ref.ly/Judg4:7) · [1KI 22:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:34) · [2CH 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:33) · [JOB 24:22](https://ref.ly/Job24:22) · [JOB 40:25](https://ref.ly/Job40:25) · [PSA 10:9](https://ref.ly/Ps10:9) · [PSA 28:3](https://ref.ly/Ps28:3) · [SNG 1:4](https://ref.ly/Song1:4) · [ISA 66:19](https://ref.ly/Isa66:19) · [JER 38:13](https://ref.ly/Jer38:13) · [EZK 32:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:20)

Associate \| verb qal

**Collocation:** משׁך יָד אֶת־לֹצְצִים

to join hands with mockers literally: to drag out (one's) hand with mockers; hence, possibly: \= action by which someone associates with mockers, ◄ described with the image of someone stretching out one's hand to someone else asking that person to join in

[HOS 7:5](https://ref.ly/Hos7:5)

Control \| verb qal

to lead literally: to draw (people); hence: \= pattern of actions by which a deity exercises leadership over humans, ◄ described with the image of someone drawing an animal with a rope

[HOS 11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:4)

Engage \| verb qal

to draw along \> to engage in literally: to draw (an activity); hence: \= action by which humans engage in a certain activity, described with the image of someone pulling a plow or cart

[ISA 5:18](https://ref.ly/Isa5:18)

Plant \| verb qal

**Collocation:** משׁך זֶרַע

to drag seed \> to sow literally: to drag seed; hence: \= action by which someone throws out seed while moving along, which makes it look as if he is dragging a trail of seed behind him

[AMO 9:13](https://ref.ly/Amos9:13)

Know \| verb qal

**Collocation:** משׁך בָּשָׂר

to tempt meaning unsure; literally: to draw (one's) body; hence, possibly: \= action by which humans try to find out how their body reacts to a certain activity

[ECC 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:3)2

Endure \| verb qal, verb nif, verb pu'al

to maintain, to continue, to prolong \= causative action by which deities cause a certain situation to last longer

[PSA 36:11](https://ref.ly/Ps36:11) · [PSA 85:6](https://ref.ly/Ps85:6) · [PSA 109:12](https://ref.ly/Ps109:12) · [PRO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Prov13:12) · [ISA 13:22](https://ref.ly/Isa13:22) · [JER 31:3](https://ref.ly/Jer31:3) · [EZK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:25) · [EZK 12:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:28)

Sounds \| verb qal

to sound a long blast \= action by which humans produce a prolonged sound with a musical instrument

[EXO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:13) · [JOS 6:5](https://ref.ly/Josh6:5)

Patient \| verb qal

to be patient, to bear with \= state in which deities tolerate a certain unacceptable situation for a certain amount of time

[NEH 9:30](https://ref.ly/Neh9:30)

1 RSV NIV NJB NRSV REB GNT ESV "tall" \| DCH "tall" or "scented" \| NJPS "far" 2 ECC 2:3 NJPS "to tempt" \| RSV NIV NRSV REB GNT ESV "to cheer" CEV "to make happy" \| NJB "to hand over"

Reinier de Blois

## משׁל (id: 004504000000000)

משׁל
====

משׁל (H4910 · H4911a · H4911b · H4914)

verb · מָשָׁל · מֹשֶׁל

Compare \| verb nif, verb hit

to be like, to become like \= state in which humans are similar in shape, behavior or experience to others

[JOB 30:19](https://ref.ly/Job30:19) · [PSA 28:1](https://ref.ly/Ps28:1) · [PSA 49:13](https://ref.ly/Ps49:13) · [PSA 49:21](https://ref.ly/Ps49:21) · [PSA 143:7](https://ref.ly/Ps143:7) · [ISA 14:10](https://ref.ly/Isa14:10)

Compare \| verb hif \| ≈ דמה · שׁוה

to compare \= action by which humans measure the similarity or dissimilarity between different entities

[ISA 46:5](https://ref.ly/Isa46:5)

Speak \| verb qal, verb pi

to speak in proverbs, to speak in riddles, to speak in parables, to speak in allegories \= action by which humans make statements to communicate something that they perceive as truth in an indirect way, such as a proverb, riddle, parable, song, or allegory; ≈ often seen as obscure and difficult to understand

[NUM 22:24](https://ref.ly/Num22:24)

noun m · שׂפח

Impact \| ≠ מִשְׁפָּט

scabs meaning unknown; possibly: \= state in which part of people's bodies is covered with crusts, possibly suggesting that they had been cut or wounded; ≈ associated with humiliation

noun m · שׂפח

Uncover \| ≠ מִשְׁפָּט

baldness meaning unknown; possibly: \= state in which part of people's bodies is no longer covered by either hair or clothes; ≈ associated with humiliation

[ISA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Isa5:7)1

noun m

Dead \| ≠ מִשְׁפָּט

bloodshed meaning unsure; possibly: \= causative action by which humans wound or kill other humans

noun m

Wrong \| ≠ מִשְׁפָּט

break of law, injustice meaning unsure; possibly: \= practices that do not conform to the laws of God

[ISA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Isa5:7)1

=========

noun m · שׁפט · עֵין מִשְׁפָּט

Conflict \| ≈ דִּין · רִיב

case, cause \= a situation where one party has a dispute with another party, and is looking for a legal ruling in which they receive what they consider to be rightfully theirs

Chastise \| ≈ יכח · נָקָם · שׂים יָד · שׁלם

=============

מִשְׁפְּתַיִם (H4942) [SAHD](https://sahd-online.com/words/mishpthayim/)

noun m · שׁפת

Stalls

sheep pen, sheepfold meaning unsure; possibly: \= area enclosed by a stone wall or hedge made of branches and thorns, with a narrow opening which can be closed with brush or by the shepherd’s body; ► used for keeping sheep at night to protect them from thieves or wild animals

[GEN 49:14](https://ref.ly/Gen49:14) · [JDG 5:16](https://ref.ly/Judg5:16)

noun m · שׁפת

Containers

saddlebasket, saddlebag meaning unsure; possibly: \= set of two large baskets ► carried by a mule or donkey on either side

[GEN 49:14](https://ref.ly/Gen49:14)

Quantity

totality literally: the weight (of a set of objects); hence: \= the totality of a set of objects

[EZR 8:30](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:30)

Quantity

**Collocation:** בְּמִשְׁקָל

by weight literally: by weight; hence: \= process by which objects are carefully weighed because of their scarcity

## מָתְנַיִם (id: 004575000000000)

מָתְנַיִם
=========

מָתְנַיִם (H4975)

noun m

loins, sides, waist \= the two sides of the body of humans and certain animals between the rib cage and the hips, ► which is the place where people normally tie a girdle, breeches, loincloth, or sackcloth; ≈ a symbol of strength

[GEN 37:34](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34) · [EXO 28:42](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42) · [2SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:8) · [1KI 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:5) · [1KI 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:10) · [1KI 20:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:31) · [1KI 20:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:32) · [2KI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:8) · [2CH 10:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:10) · [NEH 4:12](https://ref.ly/Neh4:12) · [JOB 12:18](https://ref.ly/Job12:18) · [JOB 40:16](https://ref.ly/Job40:16) · [PSA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Ps66:11) · [PSA 69:24](https://ref.ly/Ps69:24) · [ISA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:5) · [ISA 20:2](https://ref.ly/Isa20:2) · [ISA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Isa21:3) · [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1) · [JER 13:1](https://ref.ly/Jer13:1) · [JER 13:2](https://ref.ly/Jer13:2) · [JER 13:4](https://ref.ly/Jer13:4) · [JER 13:11](https://ref.ly/Jer13:11) · [JER 48:37](https://ref.ly/Jer48:37) · [EZK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:27) · [EZK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:27) · [EZK 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:2) · [EZK 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:2) · [EZK 9:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:2) · [EZK 9:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:3) · [EZK 9:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:11) · [EZK 21:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:11) · [EZK 23:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:15) · [EZK 29:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:7) · [EZK 44:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:18) · [EZK 47:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:4) · [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10) · [NAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Nah2:2) · [NAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Nah2:11)

Birds

**Collocation:** זַרְזִיר מָתְנַיִם

rooster literally: narrow waisted; exact meaning unsure; possibly: \= male variant of a dark, brownish red fowl, with orange\-red neck hackles, a smallish red comb on the top of their heads, and red lappets on each side under the beak; they have a white spot on their backs near the base of their long glossy black and green tails

[PRO 30:31](https://ref.ly/Prov30:31)1

Dress \| verb qal

**Collocation:** אזר מָתְנַיִם

to tuck (one's) cloak in (one's) belt literally: to gird (one's) loins; hence: \= action by which humans hitch up their mantle and keep it in place with a belt around their waist, ► in order to be able to walk or work

[JER 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jer1:17)

Dress \| verb qal

**Collocation:** חגר מָתְנַיִם

to tuck (one's) cloak in (one's) belt literally: to gird (one's) loins; hence: \= action by which humans hitch up their mantle and keep it in place with a belt around their waist, ► in order to be able to walk or work

[EXO 12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:11) · [2KI 4:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:29) · [2KI 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:1) · [PRO 31:17](https://ref.ly/Prov31:17) · [DAN 10:5](https://ref.ly/Dan10:5)

Dress \| verb pi

**Collocation:** שׁנס מָתְנַיִם

to tuck (one's) cloak in (one's) belt literally: to gird (one's) loins; hence: \= action by which humans hitch up their mantle and keep it in place with a belt around their waist, ► in order to be able to walk or work

[1KI 18:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:46)

Strong

strength literally: loins; hence: \= physical strength, ◄ considered to be residing in the loins of humans and certain animals

[DEU 33:11](https://ref.ly/Deut33:11)
